                                                                                                * blocked.
Actions
                                                                                                * create task;
                                                                                                * break down task;
                                                                                                * assign owner;
                                                                                                * estimate time/cost;
                                                                                                * automate where possible;
                                                                                                * follow up;
                                                                                                * close with evidence;
                                                                                                * trigger reroute if task fails.
ERP-style equivalent
This is similar to project management and planning, but deeply connected to readiness, evidence, and decision gates.
Key rule
Every recommendation should become either a decision, a task, a block, or an automation option.
________________


MODULE 12 — Operations, SOPs & Process System
Purpose
This module prepares the venture to function without constant improvisation.
It should contain
                                                                                                * operating model;
                                                                                                * workflows;
                                                                                                * SOPs;
                                                                                                * customer support flow;
                                                                                                * admin flow;
                                                                                                * delivery process;
                                                                                                * quality control;
                                                                                                * scheduling;
                                                                                                * invoicing/admin process;
                                                                                                * supplier process;
                                                                                                * delegation plan;
                                                                                                * automation plan;
                                                                                                * capacity limits.
Actions
                                                                                                * create SOP;
                                                                                                * generate checklist;
                                                                                                * identify automation opportunity;
                                                                                                * recommend tool;
                                                                                                * prepare outsourcing brief;
                                                                                                * pause growth if operations are unsafe;
                                                                                                * standardize service package.
Key rule
The system should prevent traction from turning into chaos.
________________


MODULE 13 — Documents, Knowledge & External Materials
Purpose
This module manages all documents as living outputs of the venture database.
It should contain
                                                                                                * business plan;
                                                                                                * venture brief;
                                                                                                * pitch deck;
                                                                                                * one-pager;
                                                                                                * grant summary;
                                                                                                * bank memo;
                                                                                                * partner proposal;
                                                                                                * mentor brief;
                                                                                                * customer scripts;
                                                                                                * outreach templates;
                                                                                                * public description;
                                                                                                * legal/professional prep documents;
                                                                                                * meeting agendas;
                                                                                                * summaries;
                                                                                                * uploaded files.
Document statuses
                                                                                                * draft;
                                                                                                * current;
                                                                                                * approved;
                                                                                                * speculative;
                                                                                                * internal-only;
                                                                                                * advisor-ready;
                                                                                                * professional-review-ready;
                                                                                                * partner-ready;
                                                                                                * bank/funder-ready;
                                                                                                * investor-ready;
                                                                                                * public-ready;
                                                                                                * automation-ready;
                                                                                                * needs review;
                                                                                                * outdated;
                                                                                                * blocked;
                                                                                                * superseded;
                                                                                                * archived.
Actions
                                                                                                * generate;
                                                                                                * regenerate;
                                                                                                * update from changed data;
                                                                                                * export;
                                                                                                * send with approval;
                                                                                                * compare versions;
                                                                                                * mark outdated;
                                                                                                * prepare audience-specific version.
Key rule
Documents should not be the source of truth. The structured venture records should be the source of truth, and documents should be generated views.
________________


MODULE 14 — Risk, Legal, Ethics & Compliance
Purpose
This module keeps the venture responsible.
It should contain
                                                                                                * risk register;
                                                                                                * legal review needs;
                                                                                                * tax/accounting review needs;
                                                                                                * professional-review checklist;
                                                                                                * ethical risks;
                                                                                                * data/privacy risks;
                                                                                                * vulnerable-user concerns;
                                                                                                * regulatory risks;
                                                                                                * insurance/permit needs;
                                                                                                * IP concerns;
                                                                                                * employment/contract issues;
                                                                                                * unresolved risks.
Actions
                                                                                                * block risky step;
                                                                                                * prepare professional review pack;
                                                                                                * draft questions for expert;
                                                                                                * identify professional category;
                                                                                                * update status after advice;
                                                                                                * create safer alternative route.
Key rule
Changepreneurship can prepare, organize, and route, but it must not pretend to replace licensed professional judgment.
________________


MODULE 15 — External Platforms, Marketplaces & Programs
Purpose
This module connects the venture to the outside business ecosystem.
Platform categories
                                                                                                * co-founder platforms;
                                                                                                * mentor platforms;
                                                                                                * freelancer marketplaces;
                                                                                                * grant databases;
                                                                                                * investor/startup networks;
                                                                                                * accelerator/incubator platforms;
                                                                                                * local business support organizations;
                                                                                                * research commercialization networks;
                                                                                                * social-impact networks;
                                                                                                * customer discovery/testing platforms;
                                                                                                * e-commerce/product marketplaces;
                                                                                                * hiring/collaboration platforms;
                                                                                                * legal/accounting/service-provider directories.
It should contain
                                                                                                * recommended platforms;
                                                                                                * reason for recommendation;
                                                                                                * connected account status;
                                                                                                * account creation status;
                                                                                                * profile status;
                                                                                                * applications;
                                                                                                * messages;
                                                                                                * responses;
                                                                                                * opportunities created;
                                                                                                * costs;
                                                                                                * deadlines;
                                                                                                * evidence imported.
Actions
                                                                                                * recommend platform;
                                                                                                * create account guidance;
                                                                                                * prefill profile;
                                                                                                * connect account;
                                                                                                * draft application;
                                                                                                * submit after approval;
                                                                                                * track replies;
                                                                                                * import outcomes;
                                                                                                * convert outcomes into tasks, evidence, or opportunities.
Key rule
External platforms should become connected action channels, not disconnected places where the user loses context.
________________


MODULE 16 — Human Capital: Mentors, Advisors, Co-Founders, Freelancers
Purpose
This module manages the people the venture may need before it has a team.
It should contain
                                                                                                * missing capability map;
                                                                                                * mentor profile;
                                                                                                * advisor profile;
                                                                                                * co-founder profile;
                                                                                                * freelancer role briefs;
                                                                                                * candidate lists;
                                                                                                * outreach history;
                                                                                                * evaluation criteria;
                                                                                                * interview questions;
                                                                                                * fit scores;
                                                                                                * legal/equity caution notes;
                                                                                                * onboarding tasks.
Actions
                                                                                                * define missing role;
                                                                                                * search user network;
                                                                                                * search marketplace/platform;
                                                                                                * draft outreach;
                                                                                                * send approved message;
                                                                                                * evaluate replies;
                                                                                                * prepare meeting agenda;
                                                                                                * create trial task;
                                                                                                * recommend professional review before equity or contracts.
Key rule
AI can reduce the burden, but some gaps require humans. The system should know when a human mentor, advisor, co-founder, or specialist is the correct next step.
________________


MODULE 17 — Automation, Agents & Execution Queue
Purpose
This module manages the work Changepreneurship can perform on behalf of the user.
It should contain
                                                                                                * proposed AI actions;
                                                                                                * approved AI actions;
                                                                                                * rejected AI actions;
                                                                                                * running automations;
                                                                                                * completed AI tasks;
                                                                                                * permission level;
                                                                                                * cost estimate;
                                                                                                * credit usage;
                                                                                                * data used;
                                                                                                * external platform used;
                                                                                                * output created;
                                                                                                * result;
                                                                                                * audit trail.
Permission levels
Level
	Meaning
	Suggest only
	AI recommends but does not draft or act.
	Draft only
	AI drafts; user executes manually.
	Prepare for approval
	AI prepares complete action for approval.
	Execute once approved
	AI performs one approved action.
	Execute within rules
	AI performs repeated low-risk actions within defined limits.
	Never automate
	User or professional must handle directly.
	Actions AI may perform with approval
                                                                                                * draft and send outreach;
                                                                                                * prepare applications;
                                                                                                * summarize replies;
                                                                                                * follow up;
                                                                                                * schedule meetings if connected;
                                                                                                * create tasks;
                                                                                                * update evidence log;
                                                                                                * monitor deadlines;
                                                                                                * generate documents;
                                                                                                * research platforms;
                                                                                                * compare candidates;
                                                                                                * prepare freelancer briefs;
                                                                                                * prepare mentor/co-founder profiles;
                                                                                                * organize files;
                                                                                                * produce weekly progress summaries.
Actions requiring strong approval or professional caution
                                                                                                * spending money;
                                                                                                * hiring or contracting;
                                                                                                * contacting investors/funders/banks/government bodies;
                                                                                                * posting publicly;
                                                                                                * making legal, medical, financial, or safety claims;
                                                                                                * sending sensitive personal information;
                                                                                                * creating external accounts;
                                                                                                * accepting terms;
                                                                                                * signing documents;
                                                                                                * making commitments.
Key rule
The AI may work in the venture, but the user must remain the responsible principal.
________________


MODULE 18 — Metrics, BI & Learning System
Purpose
This module turns venture activity into insight.
It should contain
                                                                                                * readiness metrics;
                                                                                                * validation metrics;
                                                                                                * outreach metrics;
                                                                                                * pipeline metrics;
                                                                                                * financial metrics;
                                                                                                * operational metrics;
                                                                                                * user workload metrics;
                                                                                                * risk metrics;
                                                                                                * impact metrics;
                                                                                                * automation ROI metrics;
                                                                                                * benchmark comparisons;
                                                                                                * trend lines;
                                                                                                * alerts.
Examples
                                                                                                * number of customer interviews completed;
                                                                                                * response rate;
                                                                                                * conversion rate;
                                                                                                * willingness-to-pay signals;
                                                                                                * pilot conversion;
                                                                                                * revenue/funding progress;
                                                                                                * runway remaining;
                                                                                                * AI credits spent;
                                                                                                * cost per useful response;
                                                                                                * tasks completed;
                                                                                                * missed deadlines;
                                                                                                * evidence quality trend;
                                                                                                * blocker resolution time;
                                                                                                * founder workload;
                                                                                                * customer satisfaction;
                                                                                                * impact indicators.
Actions
                                                                                                * trigger warning;
                                                                                                * recommend next experiment;
                                                                                                * detect stale assumptions;
                                                                                                * detect automation waste;
                                                                                                * detect lack of progress;
                                                                                                * generate weekly/monthly summary;
                                                                                                * update roadmap.
Key rule
The system should not only store actions. It should learn from them.
________________


MODULE 19 — Consumption, Credits & Cost Governance
Purpose
Because Changepreneurship may run a pay-as-you-consume model, cost control must be native to the dashboard.
It should contain
                                                                                                * subscription status;
                                                                                                * credits used;
                                                                                                * forecasted usage;
                                                                                                * cost per proposed action;
                                                                                                * monthly cap;
                                                                                                * spending approvals;
                                                                                                * free/manual alternatives;
                                                                                                * AI-assisted option;
                                                                                                * AI-executed option;
                                                                                                * external platform fees;
                                                                                                * freelancer/service-provider cost estimates;
                                                                                                * return-on-action tracking.
Example cost display
“Recommended action: send 20 mentor outreach messages. Estimated cost: 35 credits. Manual alternative: export contact list and send yourself. Expected benefit: reduce mentor-search blocker. Requires approval before sending.”
Key rule
The user should never feel that automation is a hidden meter running in the background. Cost must be visible before meaningful consumption happens.
________________


MODULE 20 — Governance, Permissions & Audit Trail
Purpose
This module protects the user from uncontrolled automation, privacy mistakes, and unclear responsibility.
It should contain
                                                                                                * connected accounts;
                                                                                                * data permissions;
                                                                                                * automation permissions;
                                                                                                * external action approvals;
                                                                                                * document-sharing approvals;
                                                                                                * account-creation approvals;
                                                                                                * consent history;
                                                                                                * revoked permissions;
                                                                                                * audit log;
                                                                                                * who/what performed each action;
                                                                                                * when action happened;
                                                                                                * what data was used;
                                                                                                * what cost was incurred;
                                                                                                * what result occurred.
Key rule
The more Changepreneurship acts on behalf of the user, the more important consent, logging, reversibility, and transparency become.
________________


MODULE 21 — Decision Log, Version History & Dependency Map
Purpose
This module preserves traceability.
It should contain
                                                                                                * major decisions;
                                                                                                * why decisions were made;
                                                                                                * evidence used;
                                                                                                * rejected alternatives;
                                                                                                * changed assumptions;
                                                                                                * affected outputs;
                                                                                                * regenerated documents;
                                                                                                * previous versions;
                                                                                                * current version;
                                                                                                * archived versions;
                                                                                                * external actions taken from each version;
                                                                                                * outdated materials;
                                                                                                * active dependencies.
Actions
                                                                                                * compare versions;
                                                                                                * restore previous version;
                                                                                                * show what changed;
                                                                                                * show affected documents;
                                                                                                * show affected tasks;
                                                                                                * export decision history;
                                                                                                * generate explanation for co-founder/mentor/investor/advisor.
Key rule
The user should always be able to understand how the venture got to its current form.
________________


MODULE 22 — Next 90 Days & Operating Rhythm
Purpose
This module keeps the venture moving after the 7 phases.
It should contain
                                                                                                * recommended next mode;
                                                                                                * 90-day objective;
                                                                                                * milestones;
                                                                                                * weekly rhythm;
                                                                                                * recurring reviews;
                                                                                                * next experiments;
                                                                                                * people to contact;
                                                                                                * budget limits;
                                                                                                * automation options;
                                                                                                * blocked actions;
                                                                                                * success criteria;
                                                                                                * stop/pivot triggers.
Next modes
                                                                                                * Operations Mode;
                                                                                                * Growth Mode;
                                                                                                * Funding Mode;
                                                                                                * Optimization Mode;
                                                                                                * Pivot Mode;
                                                                                                * Stabilization Mode;
                                                                                                * Pause Mode;
                                                                                                * Stop / Archive Mode;
                                                                                                * New Venture Mode.
Key rule
The final deliverable should never end with “good luck.” It must define the next operating rhythm.
________________


5. The lifecycle: how a block becomes action, evidence, and updated strategy
The deepest logic of the final deliverable is the loop.
A good ERP-like system does not merely hold information. It turns events into updated records and next actions.
Changepreneurship should work through this loop:
                                                                                                1. Detect
The platform detects a gap, risk, opportunity, or next step.
                                                                                                2. Explain
It explains why this matters.
                                                                                                3. Recommend
It recommends a specific action.
                                                                                                4. Offer execution modes
User does it, AI assists, or AI executes with approval.
                                                                                                5. Estimate cost and risk
The platform shows usage cost, privacy implications, and possible downsides.
                                                                                                6. Get approval
The user approves, rejects, edits, or postpones.
                                                                                                7. Execute
The action happens manually, AI-assisted, or AI-executed.
                                                                                                8. Capture result
Response, evidence, cost, and outcome are logged.
                                                                                                9. Update records
Relevant contacts, assumptions, evidence, tasks, documents, and scores update.
                                                                                                10. Recalculate route
The platform decides whether to continue, block, pivot, accelerate, or stop.
This loop is the heart of Changepreneurship.
Without this loop, the dashboard is only an information display. With this loop, it becomes an operating system.
________________


6. What makes this different from a traditional ERP?
The platform can be inspired by ERP systems, but it should not become a conventional ERP too early.
Traditional ERP
A traditional ERP usually assumes:
                                                                                                   * the company already exists;
                                                                                                   * roles already exist;
                                                                                                   * processes already exist;
                                                                                                   * departments already exist;
                                                                                                   * customers and suppliers already exist;
                                                                                                   * accounting and operations already need administration;
                                                                                                   * the main problem is managing established business processes.
Changepreneurship Venture Operating System
Changepreneurship assumes:
                                                                                                   * the venture may not exist yet;
                                                                                                   * the user may not know the right idea yet;
                                                                                                   * the customer may not be validated yet;
                                                                                                   * the business model may not be proven yet;
                                                                                                   * the founder may not be ready yet;
                                                                                                   * the first team may not exist yet;
                                                                                                   * the first mentor may not exist yet;
                                                                                                   * the first customer may not exist yet;
                                                                                                   * the system itself must help create the venture structure.
Therefore, Changepreneurship is not simply an ERP. It is a pre-ERP and early-ERP system.
It becomes more ERP-like as the venture matures.
Progression of system maturity
Venture stage
	System behaviour
	No idea yet
	Self-discovery, opportunity discovery, idea generation, readiness checks.
	Rough idea
	Idea clarification, assumptions, founder-idea fit.
	Clear idea
	Market validation, outreach, evidence capture.
	Early validation
	Business pillars, financial logic, mentor/co-founder/funder search.
	Concept tested
	Product/service design, MVP/pilot planning, operations setup.
	Prototype/pilot active
	CRM, pipeline, tasks, metrics, financial tracking, customer support.
	Revenue/operations active
	More ERP-like: invoicing, workflows, SOPs, capacity, team, CRM, finance integrations.
	Scaling
	Hand-off or integration with mature ERP/CRM/accounting systems if needed.
	This progression prevents premature complexity.
________________


7. Data model principle: documents are outputs, not the foundation
This is one of the most important design principles.
A business plan should not be the main object. A business plan should be generated from structured venture records.
A pitch deck should not be the main object. A pitch deck should be generated from the current venture data, evidence, positioning, market, financials, and traction.
An outreach message should not be random. It should be generated from the target contact, goal, current evidence, user relationship, and approved tone.
A task should not be disconnected. It should be tied to a blocker, assumption, phase, output, or opportunity.
This means the platform should avoid “document-first architecture.”
It should use:
record-first architecture with document views.
This makes the system maintainable, updateable, and internally coherent.
________________


8. External-platform integration: not links, but routed action channels
The platform should not merely show links to external websites.
External platforms should become action channels inside the operating system.
Examples:
If the user needs a co-founder
The system should:
                                                                                                   1. identify the missing capability;
                                                                                                   2. define the co-founder profile;
                                                                                                   3. warn about equity/legal risks;
                                                                                                   4. suggest relevant co-founder platforms and personal-network routes;
                                                                                                   5. prepare the user profile;
                                                                                                   6. draft outreach;
                                                                                                   7. ask for approval;
                                                                                                   8. submit or send where connected;
                                                                                                   9. track replies;
                                                                                                   10. evaluate candidates;
                                                                                                   11. create meetings;
                                                                                                   12. capture learnings;
                                                                                                   13. update team-readiness status.
If the user needs a mentor
The system should:
                                                                                                   1. identify why mentor support is blocking;
                                                                                                   2. define mentor expertise needed;
                                                                                                   3. search connected network and mentor platforms;
                                                                                                   4. prepare mentor brief;
                                                                                                   5. draft request;
                                                                                                   6. send with approval;
                                                                                                   7. schedule conversation;
                                                                                                   8. prepare agenda;
                                                                                                   9. summarize feedback;
                                                                                                   10. update venture assumptions.
If the user needs customer discovery
The system should:
                                                                                                   1. define target interview profile;
                                                                                                   2. identify contacts;
                                                                                                   3. draft messages;
                                                                                                   4. send with approval;
                                                                                                   5. track responses;
                                                                                                   6. schedule calls;
                                                                                                   7. prepare interview guide;
                                                                                                   8. capture notes;
                                                                                                   9. classify evidence;
                                                                                                   10. update market validity.
If the user needs a freelancer
The system should:
                                                                                                   1. define the task;
                                                                                                   2. create scope;
                                                                                                   3. define acceptance criteria;
                                                                                                   4. estimate budget;
                                                                                                   5. warn about risks;
                                                                                                   6. suggest marketplaces;
                                                                                                   7. prepare job post;
                                                                                                   8. shortlist candidates where possible;
                                                                                                   9. help evaluate proposals;
                                                                                                   10. manage task delivery;
                                                                                                   11. store outputs.
This is how Changepreneurship reduces user workload without removing user control.
________________


9. Permission design: automation must be powerful but bounded
The more the platform works in the venture, the more important permission design becomes.
The platform should have a permission matrix for every action type.
Action type
	Default permission requirement
	Generate internal draft
	No extra approval needed after user starts module.
	Update internal dashboard
	Allowed if based on user-approved data.
	Regenerate approved external document
	User approval required.
	Send private outreach
	Explicit approval required.
	Post publicly
	Explicit approval required.
	Contact investor/funder/bank/government
	Strong explicit approval required.
	Spend credits above threshold
	Approval required.
	Spend real money
	Approval required.
	Create external account
	Approval required.
	Accept terms or contracts
	User/professional action required.
	Sign document
	User action required.
	Give legal/tax/medical/financial conclusion
	Not allowed; refer to professional.
	Share sensitive data
	Explicit approval required.
	The platform should allow automation, but never create surprise.
________________


10. Cost model: every automated option should show the alternative
Because Changepreneurship may use a pay-as-you-consume model, every automated action should show:
                                                                                                   1. what the action is;
                                                                                                   2. why it is recommended;
                                                                                                   3. expected benefit;
                                                                                                   4. estimated cost;
                                                                                                   5. manual alternative;
                                                                                                   6. lower-cost assisted option;
                                                                                                   7. what data is used;
                                                                                                   8. what approval is needed;
                                                                                                   9. what happens after completion.
Example:
“I can search your connected network for 30 possible mentor or advisor candidates, draft personalized outreach to 10 of them, and prepare a follow-up sequence. Estimated cost: 55 credits. Manual alternative: export suggested criteria and search yourself. Recommended because mentor access is currently a blocking gap.”
This gives the user choice instead of forcing automation.
________________

12. Completion states inside the final dashboard
At the end of the 7 phases, the system should not simply say “complete.” It should assign a live completion state.
Completion state
	Meaning
	Dashboard behaviour
	Ready to operate
	Venture can continue execution.
	Move to Operations Mode.
	Ready for controlled growth
	Venture has traction and capacity.
	Move to Growth Mode with guardrails.
	Ready to seek funding
	Evidence and materials support appropriate funding route.
	Move to Funding Mode.
	Ready for pilot expansion
	First pilot worked; larger test justified.
	Create pilot expansion plan.
	Needs optimization before growth
	Venture works but operations/pricing/delivery are weak.
	Move to Optimization Mode.
	Pivot recommended
	Evidence suggests changing model, segment, or offer.
	Open Pivot Mode and preserve learning.
	Pause recommended
	Personal/financial/legal/market conditions make progress unsafe.
	Move to Stabilization or Pause Mode.
	Stop current version
	Evidence does not support continuing this path.
	Archive, summarize lessons, propose restart.
	Archive and restart
	New venture path is better.
	Preserve founder profile and create new venture workspace.
	The final deliverable is therefore not an endpoint. It is a transition into the next operating mode.
________________


13. What should the user experience feel like?
The user should not feel like they received a report.
They should feel like:
“My venture now has a control room.”
They should be able to open the dashboard and see:
                                                                                                   * what exists;
                                                                                                   * what is known;
                                                                                                   * what is unproven;
                                                                                                   * what is blocked;
                                                                                                   * who needs to be contacted;
                                                                                                   * what AI can do;
                                                                                                   * what it will cost;
                                                                                                   * what requires approval;
                                                                                                   * what the next action is;
                                                                                                   * what changed since last time;
                                                                                                   * what should not be done yet.
The user should feel supported, not overwhelmed. Powerful systems can become intimidating, so the interface should reveal complexity gradually.
The default view should be simple:
                                                                                                   1. Current venture state.
                                                                                                   2. Top blocker.
                                                                                                   3. Next action.
                                                                                                   4. AI can help with this.
                                                                                                   5. Cost and approval.
                                                                                                   6. Recent evidence.
                                                                                                   7. Next review.
Advanced views should be available, but not forced.
________________


14. What the final dashboard must not become
The final dashboard must not become:
                                                                                                   * a chaotic folder of files;
                                                                                                   * a generic ERP clone;
                                                                                                   * a CRM pretending to be a venture-building platform;
                                                                                                   * a project-management board without intelligence;
                                                                                                   * a document generator with extra buttons;
                                                                                                   * a black-box AI agent that acts without consent;
                                                                                                   * a pay-as-you-consume trap;
                                                                                                   * a spam machine for outreach;
                                                                                                   * a system that creates reputational risk;
                                                                                                   * a replacement for legal, tax, medical, financial, or regulated professional advice;
                                                                                                   * a tool that encourages users to automate responsibilities they must personally own;
                                                                                                   * a platform that hides uncertainty behind polished outputs.
The dashboard must remain intelligent, modular, ethical, consent-based, and evidence-driven.
________________


15. Quality standard for the final deliverable
The Changepreneurship Venture Operating System should meet these standards:
                                                                                                   1. Unified — all modules use the same underlying venture data.
                                                                                                   2. Modular — each business area exists as a manageable block.
                                                                                                   3. Actionable — every major insight leads to a task, decision, block, or automation option.
                                                                                                   4. Executable — AI can assist or perform real-world tasks with permission.
                                                                                                   5. Evidence-driven — assumptions and claims are tied to evidence quality.
                                                                                                   6. Permission-based — external actions require the right level of approval.
                                                                                                   7. Cost-transparent — users know when credits or money will be consumed.
                                                                                                   8. Traceable — decisions, changes, automations, and external actions are logged.
                                                                                                   9. Updateable — changes update affected blocks and documents.
                                                                                                   10. Risk-aware — legal, financial, ethical, personal, and operational risks remain visible.
                                                                                                   11. ERP-inspired, not ERP-bloated — powerful enough to operate, light enough for early-stage users.
                                                                                                   12. Human-in-the-loop — AI executes only within consent, context, and responsibility boundaries.
                                                                                                   13. Network-aware — the system helps the user identify and mobilize people around the venture.
                                                                                                   14. Marketplace-connected — external platforms become integrated action channels.
                                                                                                   15. Pre-company capable — the system works before there is a company, team, revenue, or formal structure.
                                                                                                   16. Scalable — as the venture matures, the system can integrate with or hand off to mature tools.
________________


Final answer
The final deliverable after all 7 phases should be the Changepreneurship Venture Operating System, with the Venture Operating Dashboard as the user-facing command centre. It should be inspired by ERP systems such as Odoo in the sense that it is modular, integrated, workflow-driven, and built around connected records rather than disconnected documents. However, it must be adapted for a pre-company and early-stage venture context. Its job is not only to manage an existing business, but to help create the venture itself.
The dashboard should contain modular blocks for founder readiness, idea strategy, assumptions, market evidence, contacts, outreach, opportunities, business model, finance, product/service design, tasks, operations, documents, risk, legal/professional review, external platforms, mentors, co-founders, freelancers, automation, metrics, costs, permissions, decisions, version history, and the next 90-day operating rhythm. Each block should combine information, evidence, status, actions, automation options, cost estimates, approval requirements, dependencies, and history.
The deepest distinction is that Changepreneurship should not merely help the user work at the venture through strategy and planning. It should also work in the venture by performing or assisting with real-world tasks where safe and approved: finding people, drafting outreach, contacting potential mentors, customers, partners or co-founders, preparing marketplace profiles, submitting applications, tracking responses, scheduling follow-ups, capturing evidence, updating the CRM-like network layer, and converting outcomes into decisions and tasks.
This makes the final deliverable a living venture control room. It is not a beautiful folder of documents. It is the place where the venture is understood, operated, automated, tested, updated, governed, and moved forward.
⭐ Non-negotiable: the final deliverable must be a modular, ERP-inspired, AI-executed venture operating system — not a static dashboard, not a document folder, and not a generic ERP clone. It must connect information to action, action to evidence, to updated decisions, and every external action to consent, cost, traceability, and responsibility.
	 
 
5.3
Are any outputs meant to be shared with external people (investors, banks, partners)?  If yes: which ones, what level of professionalism, and should they carry the Changepreneurship brand?
Yes. Many outputs should be designed for external sharing, but not all outputs should be shareable by default. Changepreneurship must distinguish between internal thinking material, advisor-review material, professional-review material, partner-ready material, bank/funder-ready material, investor-ready material, customer-facing material, and public-facing material.
This distinction is essential because a document that is useful for internal strategy may be dangerous if sent to an investor, bank, customer, partner, government body, or professional advisor too early. The platform should therefore treat external sharing as a controlled output state, not as a simple download button.
The guiding principle is:
External outputs should only be shareable when they are audience-appropriate, evidence-labelled, current, approved by the user, and honest about what is validated versus still assumed.
1. External sharing is part of the product, not an afterthought
Changepreneurship is not only a thinking tool. It helps users move into the real world. That means the platform must generate outputs that can support conversations with real people:
                                                                                                   * mentors;
                                                                                                   * advisors;
                                                                                                   * co-founders;
                                                                                                   * banks;
                                                                                                   * investors;
                                                                                                   * grant providers;
                                                                                                   * donors;
                                                                                                   * partners;
                                                                                                   * municipalities;
                                                                                                   * institutions;
                                                                                                   * freelancers;
                                                                                                   * suppliers;
                                                                                                   * first customers;
                                                                                                   * pilot participants;
                                                                                                   * beta testers;
                                                                                                   * legal, tax, accounting, insurance, or regulatory professionals;
                                                                                                   * accelerator/incubator programs;
                                                                                                   * social-impact organizations;
                                                                                                   * community stakeholders.
External sharing should therefore be built into the Venture Operating System. The system should not merely create documents and leave the user to decide what to do. It should recommend which document is suitable for which audience, what must be reviewed first, and whether the document is safe to send.
2. Internal-only versus external-ready
The platform should label every output according to how it may be used.
Output state
	Meaning
	Can be shared externally?
	Internal only
	Useful for founder thinking, strategy, and AI routing.
	No, unless manually exported with warning.
	Draft
	Generated but not reviewed or approved.
	No.
	Speculative
	Based partly or mostly on assumptions.
	Only with clear warning and usually only to mentors/advisors.
	Advisor-ready
	Suitable for mentor/advisor feedback.
	Yes, for review conversations.
	Professional-review ready
	Organized for lawyer, accountant, tax advisor, insurance advisor, domain expert, or regulatory expert.
	Yes, with appropriate disclaimer.
	Partner-ready
	Suitable for serious partnership or pilot discussions.
	Yes, if evidence and scope are clear.
	Bank/funder-ready
	Suitable for grant, donor, bank, subsidy, or financing discussion.
	Yes, if financial logic and evidence are adequate.
	Investor-ready
	Suitable for investor outreach.
	Yes, only if traction, market, model, team, and evidence justify it.
	Customer-facing
	Suitable for customers, users, beneficiaries, or testers.
	Yes, if claims are accurate and not overpromising.
	Public-ready
	Suitable for website, landing page, social media, press, or public announcement.
	Yes, after claims, risk, and legal checks.
	Automation-ready
	Approved for AI-assisted or AI-executed outreach.
	Yes, within user-approved rules.
	Blocked
	Cannot be finalized because evidence, review, or readiness is missing.
	No.
	Outdated
	Based on old assumptions or superseded information.
	No.
	This status system is non-negotiable. The user must never confuse a polished draft with an external-ready document.
3. Which outputs are meant to be shared externally?
The platform should support many external-facing outputs, but each has a different audience and quality requirement.
A. Mentor / advisor outputs
Intended audience
                                                                                                   * business mentors;
                                                                                                   * domain experts;
                                                                                                   * experienced founders;
                                                                                                   * strategic advisors;
                                                                                                   * personal advisors;
                                                                                                   * sector specialists;
                                                                                                   * trusted peers.
Shareable outputs
                                                                                                   * Mentor Brief;
                                                                                                   * Founder Readiness Summary;
                                                                                                   * Venture Concept Summary;
                                                                                                   * Assumption Map;
                                                                                                   * Market Validation Summary;
                                                                                                   * Problem Statement;
                                                                                                   * Key Questions for Mentor;
                                                                                                   * Decision Support Pack;
                                                                                                   * Founder-Idea Fit Summary;
                                                                                                   * Risk Map.
Professionalism level
These should be clear, concise, honest, and discussion-oriented. They do not need to be glossy. They should be transparent about uncertainty.
Tone
Direct, open, and learning-oriented.
Branding
Usually light Changepreneurship branding is acceptable, especially if the user wants to show that the output was prepared through a structured platform. However, the user should be able to remove branding or use a white-label version.
Special requirement
Mentor materials should explicitly state what kind of feedback the user is seeking. A mentor should not receive a vague business plan and be expected to guess where to help.
________________


B. Co-founder candidate outputs
Intended audience
                                                                                                   * potential co-founders;
                                                                                                   * founding team candidates;
                                                                                                   * technical/commercial/operational partners;
                                                                                                   * mission-aligned collaborators.
Shareable outputs
                                                                                                   * Co-Founder Search Brief;
                                                                                                   * Missing Capability Map;
                                                                                                   * Venture Opportunity Summary;
                                                                                                   * Founder Profile Card;
                                                                                                   * Role Expectations;
                                                                                                   * Contribution Needed;
                                                                                                   * Equity/ownership caution note;
                                                                                                   * Current Venture Status;
                                                                                                   * Why This Person Is Being Contacted;
                                                                                                   * Suggested First Conversation Agenda.
Professionalism level
High enough to show seriousness, but not so polished that it hides uncertainty. Co-founder candidates need honesty, not sales theatre.
Tone
Respectful, transparent, collaborative.
Branding
Prefer user-first branding. Changepreneurship may appear subtly as “prepared with Changepreneurship” if the user chooses. The focus should be the founder and venture, not the platform.
Special requirement
Any equity, ownership, legal, vesting, or contract language must trigger legal/professional-review warnings. The platform should not generate legally final co-founder agreements without professional review.
________________


C. Investor outputs
Intended audience
                                                                                                   * angel investors;
                                                                                                   * venture capital firms;
                                                                                                   * seed funds;
                                                                                                   * strategic investors;
                                                                                                   * startup syndicates;
                                                                                                   * accelerator investment committees.
Shareable outputs
                                                                                                   * Investor Pitch Deck;
                                                                                                   * Investor Memo;
                                                                                                   * Executive Summary;
                                                                                                   * Traction Summary;
                                                                                                   * Market Evidence Pack;
                                                                                                   * Financial Model Summary;
                                                                                                   * Use-of-Funds Plan;
                                                                                                   * Team / Founder Profile;
                                                                                                   * Risk and Mitigation Summary;
                                                                                                   * Data Room Index;
                                                                                                   * Prototype/Pilot Results;
                                                                                                   * Next Milestones;
                                                                                                   * Investor Outreach Email.
Professionalism level
Very high. Investor materials must be concise, visually clean, evidence-backed, internally consistent, and commercially credible. They should not feel AI-generated, generic, or inflated.
Tone
Confident but not exaggerated. Ambitious but evidence-grounded.
Branding
Usually no visible Changepreneurship branding on investor materials by default. Investor documents should primarily carry the venture’s own branding. If Changepreneurship branding appears, it should be subtle and optional, such as “Prepared using Changepreneurship” in small footer text or metadata.
Special requirement
Investor-ready status should be hard to obtain. The platform should not mark outputs as investor-ready unless:
                                                                                                   * market evidence is strong enough;
                                                                                                   * business model is coherent;
                                                                                                   * financial model is current;
                                                                                                   * traction or validation is credible;
                                                                                                   * team gaps are disclosed;
                                                                                                   * risks are not hidden;
                                                                                                   * claims are not exaggerated;
                                                                                                   * legal/professional caveats are addressed.
If these conditions are not met, the material can be labelled investor discussion draft, not investor-ready.
________________


D. Bank / lender / financing outputs
Intended audience
