“This document is affected by new information. I recommend updating it before external use.”
Approval-required update
These must require approval before becoming the new official version:
                                                                                                      * any externally shared document;
                                                                                                      * any document marked approved;
                                                                                                      * any document marked external-ready;
                                                                                                      * anything used for investors, banks, partners, funders, legal/tax/professional review, public launch, or customer commitments;
                                                                                                      * any document that changes promises, pricing, legal claims, financial projections, team commitments, or delivery scope.
Frozen snapshots
Once a document has been shared externally, submitted, signed, or used in a formal decision, that exact version must be preserved.
The platform may create a new updated version, but it should not rewrite history.
Example:
“Pitch Deck v3 was sent to Mentor A on 14 May. Your target customer changed on 20 May. Pitch Deck v3 is now marked superseded. A new Pitch Deck v4 can be generated, but the sent version remains preserved.”
6. Update triggers
Documents should update, regenerate, or be flagged when important source data changes.
Major triggers
Trigger
	Documents likely affected
	Target customer changes
	Business plan, pitch deck, market report, outreach scripts, financial model, partner proposals.
	Problem statement changes
	Venture concept, pitch materials, validation plan, customer messages.
	Value proposition changes
	Website copy, pitch deck, customer outreach, offer definition.
	Evidence added
	Market report, pitch deck, investor memo, validation score, evidence log.
	Evidence rejected or downgraded
	Market report, readiness labels, investor materials, business model confidence.
	Pricing changes
	Financial model, business plan, bank memo, customer offer, revenue model.
	Business model changes
	Business Pillars Blueprint, pitch deck, financial model, partner/funder materials.
	Venture type changes
	Dashboard language, funding model, legal checklist, metrics, external materials.
	Financial runway changes
	Founder readiness, spending blockers, 90-day plan, budget, hiring/development recommendations.
	Legal risk appears
	Risk register, external documents, customer claims, professional-review pack.
	Mentor feedback received
	Decision log, assumption map, strategy brief, next actions.
	Customer responses received
	Evidence log, market validity report, product concept test result.
	Product scope changes
	MVP brief, freelancer brief, development plan, cost model, customer messaging.
	Operations change
	SOPs, capacity plan, task board, growth recommendations.
	External document shared
	Document status, audit trail, recipient log, follow-up tasks.
	User returns after inactivity
	Freshness status, external-ready status, re-entry snapshot.
	The update engine should know dependencies.
7. Dependency mapping
Every document should know which records it depends on.
For example:
Pitch deck dependencies
                                                                                                      * venture concept;
                                                                                                      * target customer;
                                                                                                      * market evidence;
                                                                                                      * traction data;
                                                                                                      * business model;
                                                                                                      * team/founder profile;
                                                                                                      * financial model;
                                                                                                      * risk summary;
                                                                                                      * product roadmap;
                                                                                                      * funding ask;
                                                                                                      * latest approved positioning.
If any of these change, the pitch deck should be flagged.
Bank memo dependencies
                                                                                                      * financial model;
                                                                                                      * revenue assumptions;
                                                                                                      * cost structure;
                                                                                                      * repayment logic;
                                                                                                      * runway;
                                                                                                      * risk register;
                                                                                                      * business model;
                                                                                                      * personal financial safety where relevant.
If financial assumptions change, the bank memo should be flagged.
Grant proposal dependencies
                                                                                                      * theory of change;
                                                                                                      * impact metrics;
                                                                                                      * beneficiary evidence;
                                                                                                      * budget;
                                                                                                      * implementation plan;
                                                                                                      * reporting plan;
                                                                                                      * sustainability model;
                                                                                                      * partner commitments.
If impact metrics or budget change, the grant proposal should be flagged.
Customer outreach dependencies
                                                                                                      * target customer;
                                                                                                      * current offer;
                                                                                                      * validation goal;
                                                                                                      * tone;
                                                                                                      * user relationship to recipient;
                                                                                                      * product stage;
                                                                                                      * legal/privacy requirements.
If offer or stage changes, outreach templates should be updated.
8. Partial updates versus full regeneration
Not every change requires full regeneration. The platform should distinguish between partial updates and full document regeneration.
Partial update is enough when:
                                                                                                      * a number changes but the strategy remains the same;
                                                                                                      * a minor wording change improves clarity;
                                                                                                      * a new evidence item supports an existing conclusion;
                                                                                                      * a task status changes;
                                                                                                      * a date, metric, or contact is updated;
                                                                                                      * the user corrects a small factual detail.
Full regeneration is needed when:
                                                                                                      * target customer changes;
                                                                                                      * business model changes;
                                                                                                      * venture type changes;
                                                                                                      * pricing model changes significantly;
                                                                                                      * market evidence contradicts previous direction;
                                                                                                      * product scope changes materially;
                                                                                                      * legal risk changes the allowable route;
                                                                                                      * the user pivots;
                                                                                                      * the document is being repurposed for a different audience.
Review-only is needed when:
                                                                                                      * professional review is required;
                                                                                                      * legal, tax, financial, medical, or regulatory claims are involved;
                                                                                                      * external commitments are affected;
                                                                                                      * user judgment is needed on tone, promises, or strategy.
9. Versioning rules
Every important document should have versions.
Version labels could be:
                                                                                                      * v0.1 Draft;
                                                                                                      * v0.2 Revised Draft;
                                                                                                      * v1.0 Approved Internal;
                                                                                                      * v1.1 Updated Internal;
                                                                                                      * v2.0 External-Ready;
                                                                                                      * v2.1 Shared with Mentor;
                                                                                                      * v3.0 Investor Draft;
                                                                                                      * v3.1 Superseded;
                                                                                                      * v4.0 Current External-Ready.
Each version should store:
                                                                                                      * creation date;
                                                                                                      * source records used;
                                                                                                      * changes since previous version;
                                                                                                      * approval status;
                                                                                                      * audience;
                                                                                                      * branding mode;
                                                                                                      * external-sharing history;
                                                                                                      * author or generator;
                                                                                                      * user edits;
                                                                                                      * evidence level;
                                                                                                      * risk warnings;
                                                                                                      * export history.
This is ERP-like document control, adapted for early-stage ventures.
10. Change-impact summary
Whenever a change affects documents, the platform should show a change-impact summary.
Example:
“You changed the primary customer from individual freelancers to small agencies. This affects 7 documents: Venture Concept, Market Validity Report, Business Pillars Blueprint, Pitch Deck, Customer Outreach Script, Financial Model, and Partner Proposal. I recommend regenerating 4 and reviewing 3. Nothing external will be changed or sent without your approval.”
The summary should include:
                                                                                                      1. what changed;
                                                                                                      2. why it matters;
                                                                                                      3. affected documents;
                                                                                                      4. affected dashboard blocks;
                                                                                                      5. recommended update type;
                                                                                                      6. cost estimate if regeneration consumes credits;
                                                                                                      7. approval requirement;
                                                                                                      8. risk if ignored;
                                                                                                      9. option to update now, later, or dismiss.
11. Document refresh queue
The Venture Operating Dashboard should include a Document Refresh Queue.
It should show:
                                                                                                      * documents needing update;
                                                                                                      * reason for update;
                                                                                                      * severity;
                                                                                                      * affected audience;
                                                                                                      * last updated date;
                                                                                                      * whether document was shared externally;
                                                                                                      * recommended action;
                                                                                                      * estimated cost;
                                                                                                      * approval status.
Example queue:
Document
	Status
	Reason
	Recommended action
	Pitch Deck v3
	Needs review
	Target customer changed
	Regenerate before investor use.
	Financial Model v2
	Partially outdated
	Pricing updated
	Update pricing tab.
	Mentor Brief v1
	Current
	No material change
	No action needed.
	Grant Proposal v1
	Blocked
	Impact metrics incomplete
	Define metrics first.
	Landing Page Copy v2
	Outdated
	Offer changed
	Regenerate customer-facing copy.
	This prevents document management from becoming chaotic.
12. External sharing and updates
Externally shared documents need special handling.
When a document has been shared externally, the platform should:
                                                                                                      * preserve the exact shared version;
                                                                                                      * record who received it;
                                                                                                      * record when it was sent;
                                                                                                      * record by which channel;
                                                                                                      * record what attachments were included;
                                                                                                      * record whether follow-up is needed;
                                                                                                      * mark it as superseded if assumptions change;
                                                                                                      * warn before sending outdated materials again;
                                                                                                      * optionally notify the user if a correction/update should be sent.
The system should not automatically send updated versions to external recipients unless the user explicitly approves.
Example:
“The partner proposal sent to Gemeente X is now outdated because the pilot scope changed. I recommend sending an updated version, but I will not do so without your approval.”
13. Data room update logic
If the platform supports a Venture Data Room, updates should be handled carefully.
The user should choose whether documents in the data room are:
                                                                                                      * manually updated;
                                                                                                      * updated after approval;
                                                                                                      * updated automatically only for internal dashboards;
                                                                                                      * frozen per recipient;
                                                                                                      * versioned with change notifications;
                                                                                                      * expired after a date.
For serious stakeholders, the platform should preserve access logs and document versions.
The user should be able to say:
                                                                                                      * “Replace the old version for all viewers.”
                                                                                                      * “Add the new version but keep old version visible.”
                                                                                                      * “Send update notification to selected recipients.”
                                                                                                      * “Do not update external data room yet.”
14. User-edited documents
Users may edit AI-generated documents manually. The platform must respect user edits.
If the system regenerates a document, it should not casually overwrite manual work.
The platform should support:
                                                                                                      * locked user-edited sections;
                                                                                                      * AI-suggested edits;
                                                                                                      * compare changes;
                                                                                                      * merge updates;
                                                                                                      * preserve manual language;
                                                                                                      * user approval before replacing text;
                                                                                                      * restore previous version;
                                                                                                      * comments explaining why AI recommends change.
Example:
“You manually edited the founder story section. I will preserve your wording and only suggest updates to the market-evidence section.”
15. Living sections inside documents
A document may contain both dynamic and fixed sections.
Example: business plan
Section
	Update behaviour
	Executive summary
	Suggested update when major facts change.
	Founder story
	Manual/user-approved only.
	Market evidence
	Auto-refresh draft, approval required for official version.
	Financial projections
	Recalculate from financial model.
	Risk section
	Auto-update internally; approval required externally.
	Legal notes
	Professional-review required.
	Roadmap
	Auto-update draft from task board; user approves final.
	This hybrid model is more precise than treating the whole document as either fixed or dynamic.
16. Evidence-based document confidence
When documents update, their confidence level should update too.
Examples:
                                                                                                      * A pitch deck may move from speculative to advisor-ready after evidence improves.
                                                                                                      * A market report may move from current to outdated after 6 months of inactivity.
                                                                                                      * A business plan may move from external-ready to needs review after pricing changes.
                                                                                                      * A grant proposal may remain blocked until impact metrics are defined.
                                                                                                      * A customer offer may become ready after legal/claims review.
Confidence should be tied to evidence and freshness, not only writing quality.
17. Automatic updates over time
Some updates should happen even if the user has done nothing, because time itself can make information stale.
Examples:
                                                                                                      * market research older than 90–180 days may need review;
                                                                                                      * pitch deck older than 30–60 days should be checked before external sharing;
                                                                                                      * financial model older than 30–60 days may be stale;
                                                                                                      * competitor map in fast-moving sectors may need refresh;
                                                                                                      * legal/regulatory assumptions may need review after time or rule changes;
                                                                                                      * inactive user returning after 28+ days should trigger re-entry check.
The platform should not rewrite everything automatically. It should mark freshness risk and recommend review.
18. Automation cost and document updates
If regenerating documents consumes credits, the platform should be transparent.
Example:
“Updating the internal dashboard is free. Regenerating the full investor deck and financial summary will use approximately 45 credits. You can also update only the affected slides for 12 credits.”
The platform should offer choices:
                                                                                                      * update all affected documents;
                                                                                                      * update only internal views;
                                                                                                      * update only one document;
                                                                                                      * update only affected sections;
                                                                                                      * postpone update;
                                                                                                      * update manually;
                                                                                                      * ignore with warning.
This respects the pay-as-you-consume model.
19. User control settings
Users should be able to choose document update preferences.
Possible settings:
Setting
	Meaning
	Keep internal dashboard always current
	Internal views update automatically.
	Ask before regenerating documents
	User approval required for document regeneration.
	Auto-refresh drafts
	Draft documents update when source data changes.
	Preserve approved versions
	Approved documents never change without approval.
	Freeze shared documents
	Shared documents are permanent snapshots.
	Notify me when documents become outdated
	User receives warning.
	Batch document updates
	Save update suggestions and process together.
	Low-cost mode
	Only flag updates; do not regenerate unless requested.
	High-automation mode
	Prepare updates automatically but require approval before external use.
	The default should be safe:
Auto-update internal dashboard; flag affected documents; require approval before changing approved or external materials.
20. Developer-facing update architecture
The technical architecture should include:
                                                                                                      1. Structured source records
Documents are generated from records, not isolated text.
                                                                                                      2. Dependency graph
Every document knows which records and assumptions it depends on.
                                                                                                      3. Change detection
The system detects when source records change.
                                                                                                      4. Impact analysis
The system identifies affected documents, blocks, tasks, and external materials.
                                                                                                      5. Update policy engine
Determines auto-update, suggested update, approval-required update, frozen snapshot, or blocked update.
                                                                                                      6. Versioning system
Stores previous and current versions.
                                                                                                      7. Diff engine
Shows what changed.
                                                                                                      8. Approval workflow
User approves changes before official/external replacement.
                                                                                                      9. Cost estimator
Shows credit cost for regeneration.
                                                                                                      10. Document status tracker
Labels current, outdated, shared, blocked, etc.
                                                                                                      11. External-sharing log
Records who received which version.
                                                                                                      12. Rollback/restore
User can return to earlier versions.
                                                                                                      13. Professional-review flags
Prevents inappropriate automated updates for regulated or professional areas.
21. Examples
Example 1 — User completes Market Research
The user completes 15 customer interviews. Evidence is stronger than before.
The platform should:
                                                                                                         * update evidence log automatically;
                                                                                                         * update market dashboard automatically;
                                                                                                         * update market validity score;
                                                                                                         * flag the business plan as needing update;
                                                                                                         * prepare revised pitch deck evidence slide;
                                                                                                         * recommend updating customer messaging;
                                                                                                         * ask before replacing any approved documents.
Example 2 — User changes target customer
The user changes from “individual freelancers” to “small agencies.”
The platform should:
                                                                                                         * update the Idea Record;
                                                                                                         * mark old outreach scripts outdated;
                                                                                                         * mark market research partially outdated;
                                                                                                         * flag pricing model;
                                                                                                         * flag pitch deck;
                                                                                                         * recommend new customer discovery;
                                                                                                         * show downstream impact;
                                                                                                         * preserve old version as a previous segment hypothesis.
Example 3 — User receives mentor feedback
A mentor says the pricing is unrealistic.
The platform should:
                                                                                                         * log mentor feedback;
                                                                                                         * create evidence record;
                                                                                                         * flag pricing assumptions;
                                                                                                         * recommend pricing validation task;
                                                                                                         * mark financial model as needs review;
                                                                                                         * update decision log;
                                                                                                         * avoid automatically changing pricing without user approval.
Example 4 — User already sent investor deck
User sent Pitch Deck v2 last week. Now market evidence changed.
The platform should:
                                                                                                         * preserve Pitch Deck v2 as shared snapshot;
                                                                                                         * mark it superseded;
                                                                                                         * create Pitch Deck v3 draft;
                                                                                                         * ask whether the user wants to send an update;
                                                                                                         * prepare update message if approved.
Example 5 — User updates runway
The user’s runway drops from 6 months to 1 month.
The platform should:
                                                                                                         * update founder readiness;
                                                                                                         * update financial safety block;
                                                                                                         * trigger financial warning;
                                                                                                         * block paid development;
                                                                                                         * mark budget and 90-day plan as needing review;
                                                                                                         * not automatically update external investor/funder documents without approval.
22. What the platform should not do
The platform should not:
                                                                                                         * freeze all documents forever;
                                                                                                         * silently rewrite approved documents;
                                                                                                         * silently change externally shared materials;
                                                                                                         * let outdated documents appear current;
                                                                                                         * overwrite user-edited sections without permission;
                                                                                                         * regenerate expensive documents without cost warning;
                                                                                                         * update legal/tax/professional conclusions automatically;
                                                                                                         * send updated versions externally without approval;
                                                                                                         * treat a beautiful PDF as reliable when the source data changed;
                                                                                                         * allow multiple documents to contradict each other without warning;
                                                                                                         * make users manually hunt for outdated material;
                                                                                                         * hide which version was sent to whom.
Final answer
Generated documents should be living and updateable, not fixed once created. However, they should not update blindly or silently. Changepreneurship should use a record-first architecture: structured venture records are the source of truth, and documents are generated views of that data. Internal dashboards, scores, task lists, evidence logs, and blocker statuses can update automatically. Draft documents may auto-refresh or be regenerated easily. But approved, external-ready, professionally sensitive, or already-shared documents must be versioned, flagged, reviewed, and re-approved before replacement. Documents that have already been shared externally should become frozen historical snapshots, while newer versions can be generated and marked as superseding them.
The platform should maintain dependency mapping, document statuses, version history, a document refresh queue, change-impact summaries, cost estimates for regeneration, and approval workflows. The user should always know which documents are current, outdated, speculative, blocked, approved, external-ready, or superseded.
⭐ Non-negotiable: documents must stay connected to the living venture model, but external trust and traceability matter more than silent convenience. Internal outputs can update automatically; approved or external outputs require controlled versioning and user approval.
	 
 
5.5
The platform currently generates an entrepreneurial archetype and an overall score (0–100). Do you want to keep, redefine, or expand these? Should the numerical score be visible to users, and what does a score of 100 mean to them in plain English?
Yes, the user should receive a clear summary of their entrepreneurial strengths, weaknesses, risks, and support needs. However, this should not be presented as a rigid entrepreneurial archetype. A fixed archetype is too narrow for Changepreneurship because real users do not fit neatly into one label. Users are complex, changing, mixed, contradictory, and context-dependent. The platform must support every type and combination of user, including edge cases.
The better approach is a modular Founder Profile Matrix.
This matrix should show the user’s current strengths, weaknesses, constraints, risks, compensating factors, and recommended support structure across multiple dimensions. It should be dynamic, evidence-based, and updateable over time. It should help the user understand how they are likely to operate as a founder without trapping them in a simplistic identity label.
The guiding principle is:
Do not tell the user what “type” of entrepreneur they are. Show them the current configuration of strengths, weaknesses, risks, and support needs that affects how they should build.
1. Why rigid archetypes are not suitable
Entrepreneurial archetypes can be useful for simple storytelling, but they are dangerous if treated as serious diagnostics. A user may be a visionary in one context, a cautious operator in another, technically brilliant in one domain, commercially weak in another, resilient under personal pressure but avoidant with sales, financially disciplined but socially hesitant, or highly creative but poor at follow-through.
A single archetype cannot capture that.
Rigid archetypes create several problems:
                                                                                                         * They oversimplify the user.
                                                                                                         * They make people identify with a fixed label.
                                                                                                         * They can become self-fulfilling.
                                                                                                         * They hide contradictions.
                                                                                                         * They fail to capture hybrid profiles.
                                                                                                         * They do not adapt when the user changes.
                                                                                                         * They may flatter or discourage too easily.
                                                                                                         * They do not tell the user what to do next.
                                                                                                         * They are often too broad to support routing decisions.
For example, calling someone “The Visionary” may feel encouraging, but it does not explain whether they have enough runway, whether they avoid customer conversations, whether they need a technical co-founder, whether they can sell, whether they are overbuilding, or whether the venture fits their life.
Changepreneurship needs something more precise.
2. The alternative: Founder Profile Matrix
Instead of an archetype, the user should receive a Founder Profile Matrix.
This matrix should be a structured, modular summary of the user’s founder configuration.
It should answer:
                                                                                                         1. What are this user’s strongest founder assets?
                                                                                                         2. What weaknesses or constraints create risk?
                                                                                                         3. Which weaknesses are currently covered by compensating strengths?
                                                                                                         4. Which weaknesses are uncovered and need action?
                                                                                                         5. What type of support does the user need?
                                                                                                         6. Which venture paths fit the user best?
                                                                                                         7. Which paths are dangerous or unrealistic for the user right now?
                                                                                                         8. What should the user personally do?
                                                                                                         9. What should the user delegate, automate, or find human help for?
                                                                                                         10. How should the platform route and communicate with this user?
This is more useful than a label because it produces action.
3. Recommended name
Possible names:
                                                                                                         * Founder Profile Matrix
                                                                                                         * Founder Operating Matrix
                                                                                                         * Founder Configuration Map
                                                                                                         * Entrepreneurial Readiness Matrix
                                                                                                         * Founder Strengths & Risk Matrix
                                                                                                         * Founder Capability Matrix
                                                                                                         * Founder Fit Matrix
The recommended name is:
Founder Operating Matrix
This name is strong because it suggests practical use. It is not about personality entertainment. It is about how the founder should operate.
4. What the matrix should contain
The Founder Operating Matrix should include several modular layers.
Layer 1 — Strengths
This layer shows what the user can rely on.
Possible strength areas:
                                                                                                         * domain knowledge;
                                                                                                         * resilience;
                                                                                                         * analytical thinking;
                                                                                                         * creativity;
                                                                                                         * technical skill;
                                                                                                         * communication;
                                                                                                         * sales ability;
                                                                                                         * operational discipline;
                                                                                                         * financial discipline;
                                                                                                         * network access;
                                                                                                         * learning speed;
                                                                                                         * strategic thinking;
                                                                                                         * emotional connection to the problem;
                                                                                                         * leadership ability;
                                                                                                         * persistence;
                                                                                                         * community-building ability;
                                                                                                         * ethics and values clarity;
                                                                                                         * execution consistency;
                                                                                                         * ability to use AI and automation;
                                                                                                         * ability to attract support.
The matrix should not simply say “strong.” It should say where the strength matters.
Example:
“You show strong analytical ability. This is useful for financial modelling and risk assessment, but it does not replace customer validation.”
Layer 2 — Weaknesses and gaps
This layer shows what may undermine progress.
Possible weakness areas:
                                                                                                         * low financial runway;
                                                                                                         * limited time;
                                                                                                         * weak sales ability;
                                                                                                         * no technical skill;
                                                                                                         * no domain expertise;
                                                                                                         * weak market access;
                                                                                                         * low confidence;
                                                                                                         * weak follow-through;
                                                                                                         * poor operational structure;
                                                                                                         * limited network;
                                                                                                         * avoidance of customer contact;
                                                                                                         * overthinking;
                                                                                                         * impulsive decision-making;
                                                                                                         * weak pricing logic;
                                                                                                         * lack of legal awareness;
                                                                                                         * unclear communication;
                                                                                                         * burnout risk;
                                                                                                         * discomfort with money;
                                                                                                         * poor evidence standards;
                                                                                                         * tendency to overbuild;
                                                                                                         * reluctance to delegate.
The matrix should show whether each weakness is:
                                                                                                         * minor;
                                                                                                         * important;
                                                                                                         * blocking;
                                                                                                         * temporary;
                                                                                                         * structural;
                                                                                                         * compensated;
                                                                                                         * uncompensated;
                                                                                                         * improving;
                                                                                                         * worsening;
                                                                                                         * unknown.
Layer 3 — Constraints
Constraints are not always weaknesses. They are real conditions that shape the correct route.
Examples:
                                                                                                         * limited weekly time;
                                                                                                         * caregiving duties;
                                                                                                         * employment restrictions;
                                                                                                         * location;
                                                                                                         * language barriers;
                                                                                                         * immigration or work-status constraints;
                                                                                                         * health/energy limits;
                                                                                                         * debt;
                                                                                                         * lack of transport;
                                                                                                         * lack of equipment;
                                                                                                         * limited digital literacy;
                                                                                                         * family expectations;
                                                                                                         * cultural context;
                                                                                                         * legal boundaries;
                                                                                                         * no access to capital.
The platform should not shame constraints. It should route around them.
Example:
“Your available time is limited, so the recommended route is low-intensity validation, not active product development.”
Layer 4 — Risks
Risks are conditions that may harm the user or venture if ignored.
Risk areas:
                                                                                                         * financial harm;
                                                                                                         * burnout;
                                                                                                         * legal exposure;
                                                                                                         * reputational risk;
                                                                                                         * overbuilding;
                                                                                                         * market rejection;
                                                                                                         * weak evidence;
                                                                                                         * operational collapse;
                                                                                                         * premature hiring;
                                                                                                         * premature borrowing;
                                                                                                         * public overclaiming;
                                                                                                         * AI-generated false confidence;
                                                                                                         * mission drift;
                                                                                                         * founder-role mismatch.
Risks should link directly to action blocks and warnings.
Example:
“Risk: You may spend before validation. Action: paid development remains blocked until customer evidence improves.”
Layer 5 — Compensating factors
The matrix should show where a weakness is covered by another strength or support mechanism.
Examples:
                                                                                                         * low technical skill + technical co-founder;
                                                                                                         * low business experience + strong mentor;
                                                                                                         * low confidence + consistent task completion;
                                                                                                         * low financial runway + low-capital validation route;
                                                                                                         * weak communication + strong scripts and practice;
                                                                                                         * weak network + strong outreach discipline;
                                                                                                         * weak operations + simple model and automation;
                                                                                                         * low founder-market fit + insider advisor;
                                                                                                         * high complexity + reduced MVP scope.
This layer should make compensation visible but conditional.
Example:
“Technical skill is weak but currently covered by a no-code MVP route. Custom development remains blocked until stronger validation or technical support exists.”
Layer 6 — Uncovered gaps
The matrix should clearly show what is not yet covered.
Examples:
                                                                                                         * “No credible technical execution plan.”
                                                                                                         * “No access to target customers.”
                                                                                                         * “No mentor in regulated sector.”
                                                                                                         * “No funding model.”
                                                                                                         * “No operational capacity for growth.”
                                                                                                         * “No evidence of willingness to pay.”
Uncovered gaps should become task blocks in the dashboard.
Layer 7 — Founder-role recommendation
Instead of giving one archetype, the platform should recommend what role the founder is currently best suited to play.
Possible role recommendations:
                                                                                                         * lead founder;
                                                                                                         * technical founder;
                                                                                                         * commercial founder;
                                                                                                         * domain founder;
                                                                                                         * community founder;
                                                                                                         * operations founder;
                                                                                                         * creative/product founder;
                                                                                                         * research/invention founder;
                                                                                                         * impact/mission founder;
                                                                                                         * founder with co-founder required;
                                                                                                         * founder with mentor required;
                                                                                                         * founder best suited to side-hustle route;
                                                                                                         * founder best suited to licensing/partnership route;
                                                                                                         * founder best suited to operator role, not CEO role yet;
                                                                                                         * founder should not be sole decision-maker in current venture.
These should not be identity labels. They should be current operating recommendations.
Example:
“You may be suitable as the domain founder, but not as the sole commercial lead. A sales or business-development partner is strongly recommended.”
Layer 8 — Recommended operating style
The matrix should recommend how the user should work.
Examples:
                                                                                                         * fast validation with strict financial limits;
                                                                                                         * slow and structured transition from employment;
                                                                                                         * weekly mentor review;
                                                                                                         * low-capital side-hustle testing;
                                                                                                         * small manual MVP before software;
                                                                                                         * operations cleanup before growth;
                                                                                                         * strong accountability rhythm;
                                                                                                         * no high-stakes decisions during stress;
                                                                                                         * customer conversations before documents;
                                                                                                         * outsource only after scope is validated;
                                                                                                         * use AI for admin but user must handle customer learning personally.
This makes the matrix practical.
Layer 9 — Communication and coaching style
The platform should adapt its communication to the user’s profile.
Examples:
User pattern
	Platform communication style
	Overexcited and risky
	Direct, grounding, safety-focused.
	Analytical and cautious
	Evidence-based, precise, action-forcing.
	Low confidence
	Encouraging but still concrete.
	Avoidant
	Clear task accountability.
	High expertise
	Advanced, concise, no beginner explanations.
	Impact-driven
	Mission-respecting but sustainability-focused.
	Technical/deep-tech
	Translate technical value into buyer language.
	Burnout-prone
	Slower pace, workload awareness, pressure reduction.
	Overconfident
	Evidence demands and reality checks.
	The matrix should help the AI co-founder speak in the way that is most useful for the user.
Layer 10 — Evolution over time
The Founder Operating Matrix should evolve.
It should update when:
                                                                                                         * the user completes tasks;
                                                                                                         * financial situation changes;
                                                                                                         * time availability changes;
                                                                                                         * evidence improves;
                                                                                                         * mentor feedback arrives;
                                                                                                         * the user avoids or completes validation;
                                                                                                         * a co-founder joins;
                                                                                                         * the venture pivots;
                                                                                                         * operational demands change;
                                                                                                         * the user burns out or stabilizes;
                                                                                                         * the user gains skills;
                                                                                                         * the user proves execution ability;
                                                                                                         * the user starts getting customers.
The profile should never be treated as permanent.
Example:
“Your communication score improved after three successful customer interviews. Customer outreach is no longer a major blocker, but pricing confidence remains weak.”
5. Matrix dimensions
The matrix should use the major taxonomy dimensions, but present them in a manageable way.
Recommended dimensions:
Dimension
	What it tells the user
	Personal Readiness
	Whether the user can handle the pressure of the current route.
	Financial Readiness
	Whether the user can safely absorb risk.
	Time & Capacity
	Whether the user can execute the next stage.
	Motivation & Purpose
	Whether motivation is durable and healthy.
	Skills & Experience
	Whether the user has or can access required capability.
	Founder-Idea Fit
	Whether this idea suits the user.
	Founder-Market Fit
	Whether the user can access and understand the market.
	Execution Behaviour
	Whether the user actually completes real-world tasks.
	Communication Ability
	Whether the user can explain, sell, persuade, or ask for help.
	Learning & Adaptability
	Whether the user improves after evidence and feedback.
	Support Network
	Whether the user has or can build human support.
	Risk Awareness
	Whether the user recognizes and manages risk.
	Evidence Discipline
	Whether the user distinguishes belief from proof.
	Operational Discipline
	Whether the user can build systems and avoid chaos.
	Automation Leverage
	Whether the user can use AI/tools effectively without losing control.
	Each dimension should show:
                                                                                                         * current status;
                                                                                                         * evidence behind the status;
                                                                                                         * trend;
                                                                                                         * danger zone;
                                                                                                         * blocker status;
                                                                                                         * compensating factors;
                                                                                                         * recommended action.
6. Matrix display format
The user-facing display should be clear and not overwhelming.
A good matrix row could look like this:
Dimension
	Current status
	Trend
	Risk
	Compensation
	Next action
	Financial Readiness
	Hard block
	Worsening
	Paid development unsafe
	None yet
	Complete runway plan.
	Technical Capability
	Weak but covered
	Stable
	Custom build risk
	No-code MVP route
	Test manually first.
	Market Access
	Weak
	Unknown
	No customer reach
	Possible LinkedIn contacts
	Create outreach list.
	Motivation
	Strong
	Stable
	Over-urgency
	Grounding tasks
	Define 30-day pace.
	Execution
	Promising
	Improving
	Needs proof
	Completed 3 tasks
	Complete 10 interviews.
	This is better than an archetype because it gives the user action and context.
7. Optional pattern labels, not archetypes
The platform may use pattern labels if helpful, but they should be secondary, optional, and non-rigid.
For example, instead of saying:
“You are The Visionary.”
The platform could say:
“Your current profile shows a strong vision pattern combined with weak validation evidence and limited financial runway.”
This makes the pattern modular.
Possible pattern tags:
                                                                                                         * Vision strength;
                                                                                                         * Technical depth;
                                                                                                         * Commercial gap;
                                                                                                         * Low-capital constraint;
                                                                                                         * Market-access gap;
                                                                                                         * Operational overload;
                                                                                                         * Evidence weakness;
                                                                                                         * Strong domain fit;
                                                                                                         * Burnout watch;
                                                                                                         * Mentor-needed;
                                                                                                         * Co-founder-needed;
                                                                                                         * Execution improving;
                                                                                                         * Overanalysis risk;
                                                                                                         * Overconfidence risk;
                                                                                                         * Impact-driven profile;
                                                                                                         * Deep-tech translation need;
                                                                                                         * Side-hustle route;
                                                                                                         * Financial stabilization needed.
Users can have many tags at the same time. Tags can appear, disappear, strengthen, or weaken.
This is more flexible than archetypes.
8. Edge cases the matrix must handle
The Founder Operating Matrix should support unusual and mixed profiles.
Examples:
Edge case 1 — Brilliant but unstable
Strong technical or strategic ability, but low personal stability, burnout, or financial danger.
Platform response:
                                                                                                         * recognize brilliance;
                                                                                                         * block high-pressure action;
                                                                                                         * route to stabilization;
                                                                                                         * preserve idea and capability;
                                                                                                         * do not flatter into risk.
Edge case 2 — High traction but weak founder readiness
The market wants the product, but the user is overloaded or personally unstable.
Platform response:
                                                                                                         * acknowledge demand;
                                                                                                         * block growth;
                                                                                                         * create operations/support plan;
                                                                                                         * recommend delegation, co-founder, or pause.
Edge case 3 — Weak skills but strong network
The user lacks direct ability but can mobilize people.
Platform response:
                                                                                                         * treat network as real leverage;
                                                                                                         * create mentor/co-founder/freelancer blocks;
                                                                                                         * require accountability and coordination capacity.
Edge case 4 — Strong mission but anti-money mindset
The user has impact passion but avoids funding logic.
Platform response:
                                                                                                         * respect mission;
                                                                                                         * reframe money as sustainability;
                                                                                                         * create funding/stakeholder model;
                                                                                                         * block vague impact claims.
Edge case 5 — Very high confidence, very low evidence
The user sounds persuasive but lacks proof.
Platform response:
                                                                                                         * require validation;
                                                                                                         * block investor/customer claims;
                                                                                                         * ask challenge questions;
                                                                                                         * mark outputs speculative.
Edge case 6 — Strong evidence, low confidence
The user has real demand but doubts themselves.
Platform response:
                                                                                                         * show evidence clearly;
                                                                                                         * create small next steps;
                                                                                                         * avoid false reassurance;
                                                                                                         * use real proof to build confidence.
Edge case 7 — Great founder, wrong idea
The user is capable, but the venture does not fit their market, life, values, or resources.
Platform response:
                                                                                                         * preserve founder strengths;
                                                                                                         * recommend pivot or new idea;
                                                                                                         * do not tell the user they are weak;
                                                                                                         * separate founder potential from idea fit.
Edge case 8 — Weak founder today, strong future potential
The user lacks stability now but has learning ability and motivation.
Platform response:
                                                                                                         * say not ready right now;
                                                                                                         * create preparation track;
                                                                                                         * monitor improvement;
                                                                                                         * allow re-entry when readiness improves.
Edge case 9 — Strong user but harmful venture direction
The user has skills and resources, but the venture is unethical, exploitative, or unlawful.
Platform response:
                                                                                                         * block harmful direction;
                                                                                                         * do not let strengths compensate;
                                                                                                         * offer ethical redesign if possible.
Edge case 10 — User changes dramatically over time
The user starts weak, learns quickly, gains support, validates demand, and becomes ready.
Platform response:
                                                                                                         * update matrix;
                                                                                                         * remove old blockers;
                                                                                                         * preserve learning history;
                                                                                                         * accelerate when justified.
9. The matrix should produce routing decisions
The Founder Operating Matrix should not be decorative. It should affect the operating system.
It should influence:
                                                                                                         * which phase the user enters;
                                                                                                         * which blocks appear in the dashboard;
                                                                                                         * which actions are allowed;
                                                                                                         * which actions are blocked;
                                                                                                         * whether the user can fast-track;
                                                                                                         * whether the user needs stabilization;
                                                                                                         * whether mentor/co-founder support is required;
                                                                                                         * what documents can be external-ready;
                                                                                                         * what AI can execute;
