                                                                                                                  * automation permission updates;
                                                                                                                  * user confirmation flows;
                                                                                                                  * revalidation tasks;
                                                                                                                  * score history;
                                                                                                                  * trend display;
                                                                                                                  * stale-data dashboard labels;
                                                                                                                  * audit trail for automatic changes.
26. What the platform should not do
The platform should not:
                                                                                                                  * silently reduce scores without explanation;
                                                                                                                  * punish inactivity with arbitrary score decay;
                                                                                                                  * pretend old market evidence is current;
                                                                                                                  * rely on stale financial data for spending recommendations;
                                                                                                                  * keep external-ready documents marked current after long inactivity;
                                                                                                                  * automatically change subjective personal scores without user confirmation;
                                                                                                                  * treat all sectors as aging at the same speed;
                                                                                                                  * use fake precision when it only has uncertainty;
                                                                                                                  * hide why a score changed;
                                                                                                                  * let stale scores unlock high-risk actions.
Final answer
Yes, scores should be able to change over time even if the user has done nothing new, but not through crude automatic decay. Changepreneurship should use a dynamic score freshness system. Some dimensions remain stable, such as past experience, values, and historical lessons. Other dimensions become stale quickly, such as financial runway, time availability, market evidence, competitor analysis, pricing assumptions, legal/regulatory assumptions, external-ready documents, and task roadmaps. When time passes, the platform should usually reduce confidence or mark information as stale before changing the actual score. If reliable data shows that reality has changed, then the score itself may update.
For inactivity, especially after 28 days, 90 days, 180 days, or more, the platform should trigger re-entry checks rather than blindly continuing. For market changes, the platform should flag affected scores, documents, assumptions, and actions. Every automatic score or freshness change must explain what changed, why, what it affects, and what the user must do to restore confidence or unlock progress.
⭐ Non-negotiable: scores should remain connected to reality over time, but the platform must distinguish between “this got worse,” “this may be stale,” and “we need new confirmation.” Time should trigger revalidation, not arbitrary punishment.
	 
 
6.3
Which industry sectors and founder types should the platform support at launch?  This determines how specific the peer benchmarks can be. List all sectors.
At launch, Changepreneurship should support a broad range of industry sectors and founder types, but it should not pretend that every sector has equally reliable benchmark data from day one. The correct launch strategy is to support many sectors at the routing and guidance level, while making peer benchmarks more specific only where enough comparable data exists.
The platform should therefore use a tiered sector and founder-type support model.
The guiding principle is:
Support broad venture diversity at launch, but make benchmark confidence proportional to the amount and quality of comparable data.
This means the platform can help a user in many sectors immediately, but it should clearly label whether the benchmark is strong, moderate, directional, or not yet available.
1. Why sector and founder type matter
Sector and founder type affect almost everything in Changepreneurship:
                                                                                                                  * what questions should be asked;
                                                                                                                  * which dimensions matter most;
                                                                                                                  * which risks are blocking;
                                                                                                                  * which benchmarks are relevant;
                                                                                                                  * which documents are needed;
                                                                                                                  * which external platforms are useful;
                                                                                                                  * what the correct business model may be;
                                                                                                                  * whether professional review is required;
                                                                                                                  * how fast market evidence becomes stale;
                                                                                                                  * whether the user needs a mentor, co-founder, freelancer, advisor, or regulated professional;
                                                                                                                  * whether the venture should be bootstrapped, grant-funded, donation-supported, revenue-first, VC-funded, licensed, franchised, or partnership-led.
A local gardening service, a deep-tech patent spin-out, a social-impact non-profit, a marketplace, a SaaS product, and a restaurant do not need the same benchmark logic.
2. Benchmarking should start broad and become narrow over time
At launch, the platform should not overclaim precision. If there are not enough users in a specific sector, the platform should use broader benchmark groups.
Example:
A user building an AI-powered inventory tool for small bakeries may not yet have enough direct peers in the platform. The benchmark hierarchy could be:
                                                                                                                  1. AI/software venture benchmark.
                                                                                                                  2. B2B SaaS benchmark.
                                                                                                                  3. Small-business operations software benchmark.
                                                                                                                  4. Food/hospitality customer segment benchmark.
                                                                                                                  5. Exact niche benchmark only when enough data exists.
This gives the user useful context without pretending to have perfect precision.
3. Benchmark confidence levels
Every sector/founder benchmark should have a confidence label.
Benchmark confidence
	Meaning
	User-facing wording
	Strong benchmark
	Enough comparable users and stable outcome data.
	“Compared with similar users in this category…”
	Moderate benchmark
	Some comparable users, but still limited or mixed.
	“Based on available comparable cases…”
	Directional benchmark
	Useful but broad or early.
	“This is a directional benchmark, not a precise comparison.”
	General benchmark
	Based on broad early-stage venture patterns.
	“Using general early-stage venture standards…”
	Insufficient benchmark
	Not enough comparable data yet.
	“There is not enough benchmark data for this specific niche yet.”
	The platform should not hide uncertainty. Benchmark transparency is part of trust.
4. Launch support tiers
The platform should classify sectors into launch support tiers.
Tier
	Meaning
	Platform behaviour
	Tier 1 — Full launch support
	The sector can be supported with strong routes, documents, questions, and likely useful benchmarks.
	Provide sector-specific routing and benchmarks where possible.
	Tier 2 — Supported with directional benchmarks
	The sector can be supported, but peer benchmarks may be broad at launch.
	Provide guidance and label benchmarks as directional.
	Tier 3 — Supported with professional-review gates
	The sector can be supported only with legal, ethical, safety, or compliance caution.
	Require professional-review blocks where needed.
	Tier 4 — Experimental / specialist support
	The sector is complex, uncommon, or technically specialized.
	Require mentors/advisors and avoid overconfident benchmarking.
	Tier 5 — Not supported in current form
	The venture direction is illegal, harmful, exploitative, deceptive, or outside ethical boundaries.
	Refuse or require redesign.
	This allows broad support without reckless precision.
5. Recommended launch sector taxonomy
Changepreneurship should not rely only on a traditional government industry code system. Official sector systems are useful for structure, but startup and early-venture categories often cut across them.
The platform should use a practical hybrid taxonomy:
                                                                                                                  1. Official economic sector mapping
Useful for data compatibility, statistics, banking, grants, and formal classification.
                                                                                                                  2. Venture-type sector families
Useful for product routing, benchmarking, business-model logic, and user understanding.
                                                                                                                  3. Risk/regulation overlays
Useful for legal, ethical, safety, data, financial, and professional-review triggers.
                                                                                                                  4. Business-model overlays
Useful for distinguishing SaaS, marketplace, local service, non-profit, e-commerce, agency, licensing, grant-funded, etc.
This is more useful than forcing every user into one rigid sector box.
________________


A. All launch sector families
The platform should support the following broad sector families at launch.
1. Local services and trades
Includes:
                                                                                                                     * gardening;
                                                                                                                     * cleaning;
                                                                                                                     * repairs;
                                                                                                                     * maintenance;
                                                                                                                     * handyman services;
                                                                                                                     * painting;
                                                                                                                     * plumbing;
                                                                                                                     * electrical work;
                                                                                                                     * landscaping;
                                                                                                                     * moving services;
                                                                                                                     * personal errands;
                                                                                                                     * local installation services;
                                                                                                                     * field services;
                                                                                                                     * home improvement;
                                                                                                                     * small contractor services.
Why support at launch:
                                                                                                                     * common entrepreneurial entry point;
                                                                                                                     * practical for side-hustlers;
                                                                                                                     * clear operational needs;
                                                                                                                     * strong relevance for users with practical skills;
                                                                                                                     * benchmarkable through pricing, capacity, local demand, and workload.
Special platform focus:
                                                                                                                     * pricing;
                                                                                                                     * scheduling;
                                                                                                                     * route planning;
                                                                                                                     * customer communication;
                                                                                                                     * quoting/invoicing;
                                                                                                                     * local marketing;
                                                                                                                     * capacity limits;
                                                                                                                     * burnout prevention;
                                                                                                                     * insurance/permit checks where relevant.
Launch support tier:
Tier 1 — Full launch support.
________________


2. Professional services and consulting
Includes:
                                                                                                                     * business consulting;
                                                                                                                     * marketing consulting;
                                                                                                                     * HR consulting;
                                                                                                                     * finance consulting;
                                                                                                                     * strategy consulting;
                                                                                                                     * coaching;
                                                                                                                     * training;
                                                                                                                     * writing and editing;
                                                                                                                     * design services;
                                                                                                                     * research services;
                                                                                                                     * virtual assistance;
                                                                                                                     * bookkeeping-adjacent services;
                                                                                                                     * project management services;
                                                                                                                     * operations consulting.
Why support at launch:
                                                                                                                     * common founder route;
                                                                                                                     * low capital requirements;
                                                                                                                     * clear validation through client conversations;
                                                                                                                     * strong fit for experienced professionals;
                                                                                                                     * good for bootstrap-first models.
Special platform focus:
                                                                                                                     * offer packaging;
                                                                                                                     * authority building;
                                                                                                                     * lead generation;
                                                                                                                     * pricing;
                                                                                                                     * testimonials;
                                                                                                                     * client pipeline;
                                                                                                                     * service boundaries;
                                                                                                                     * time capacity;
                                                                                                                     * founder credibility.
Launch support tier:
Tier 1 — Full launch support.

                                                                                                                     * internal business tools.

                                                                                                                     * strategically important;

                                                                                                                     * many users will want software ventures;
                                                                                                                     * strong need for evidence gates because users often overbuild.

Launch support tier:
Tier 1 for general SaaS/automation guidance; Tier 2 or Tier 4 for deep technical niches; Tier 3 when data, privacy, security, finance, health, or regulated claims are involved.

25. Other / hybrid / unknown sectors
The platform should support users who do not fit neatly into any category.
Includes:
                                                                                                                     * hybrid ventures;
                                                                                                                     * unusual combinations;
                                                                                                                     * new categories;
                                                                                                                     * multi-sector ideas;
                                                                                                                     * experimental models;
                                                                                                                     * personal inventions;
                                                                                                                     * unclear ideas;
                                                                                                                     * sector-agnostic services;
                                                                                                                     * future industries.
Launch support tier:
Supported with general venture logic and directional benchmarks until classified more precisely.
The platform should not force a false category too early. It should allow “unknown / hybrid” and then refine as the venture becomes clearer.
________________


B. Official sector mapping
For backend compatibility, the platform should eventually map each venture to formal economic sector systems where useful.
This is especially relevant for:
                                                                                                                     * banks;
                                                                                                                     * public programs;
                                                                                                                     * government statistics;
                                                                                                                     * grants;
                                                                                                                     * industry benchmarking;
                                                                                                                     * insurance;
                                                                                                                     * tax/accounting categorization;
                                                                                                                     * formal business registration;
                                                                                                                     * risk classification.
A practical backend mapping should include broad official sectors such as:
                                                                                                                     * agriculture, forestry, fishing and hunting;
                                                                                                                     * mining, quarrying, and oil/gas extraction;
                                                                                                                     * utilities;
                                                                                                                     * construction;
                                                                                                                     * manufacturing;
                                                                                                                     * wholesale trade;
                                                                                                                     * retail trade;
                                                                                                                     * transportation and warehousing;
                                                                                                                     * information;
                                                                                                                     * finance and insurance;
                                                                                                                     * real estate and rental/leasing;
                                                                                                                     * professional, scientific, and technical services;
                                                                                                                     * management of companies;
                                                                                                                     * administrative/support/waste/remediation services;
                                                                                                                     * educational services;
                                                                                                                     * health care and social assistance;
                                                                                                                     * arts, entertainment, and recreation;
                                                                                                                     * accommodation and food services;
                                                                                                                     * other services;
                                                                                                                     * public administration.
However, these official categories are not sufficient for the user interface. They are too formal and often too broad. The user-facing taxonomy should remain venture-friendly, while the backend can map to official sectors.
________________


C. Sectors that require caution or restricted support
Some sectors can be supported only with strong guardrails.
Professional-review sectors
These should trigger professional-review gates when relevant:
                                                                                                                     * medical and health claims;
                                                                                                                     * mental health services;
                                                                                                                     * financial advice, investment, lending, insurance;
                                                                                                                     * legal services or legal advice;
                                                                                                                     * tax/accounting advice;
                                                                                                                     * childcare and safeguarding-sensitive ventures;
                                                                                                                     * eldercare and disability care;
                                                                                                                     * food safety;
                                                                                                                     * transportation and safety-critical services;
                                                                                                                     * construction/permits;
                                                                                                                     * real estate/legal contracts;
                                                                                                                     * cybersecurity/security claims;
                                                                                                                     * regulated education or certification;
                                                                                                                     * employment and recruitment;
                                                                                                                     * data-heavy or privacy-sensitive products;
                                                                                                                     * environmental/carbon/green claims;
                                                                                                                     * public-sector procurement;
                                                                                                                     * IP/patent commercialization.
High-capital sectors
These should trigger financial and feasibility gates:
                                                                                                                     * manufacturing;
                                                                                                                     * hardware;
                                                                                                                     * restaurants;
                                                                                                                     * physical retail;
                                                                                                                     * real estate;
                                                                                                                     * logistics/fleet;
                                                                                                                     * inventory-heavy e-commerce;
                                                                                                                     * energy/infrastructure;
                                                                                                                     * deep-tech;
                                                                                                                     * regulated product development;
                                                                                                                     * franchising.
High-reputation-risk sectors
These should trigger external-sharing and claims checks:
                                                                                                                     * public campaigns;
                                                                                                                     * health/wellness;
                                                                                                                     * financial outcomes;
                                                                                                                     * social-impact claims;
                                                                                                                     * environmental claims;
                                                                                                                     * legal/civic issues;
                                                                                                                     * vulnerable-user services;
                                                                                                                     * public-sector partnerships;
                                                                                                                     * education credentials.
Not-supported directions
The platform should not support ventures that depend on:
                                                                                                                     * illegal activity;
                                                                                                                     * fraud;
                                                                                                                     * deception;
                                                                                                                     * exploitation;
                                                                                                                     * harassment;
                                                                                                                     * hate or discrimination;
                                                                                                                     * privacy invasion;
                                                                                                                     * unsafe products;
                                                                                                                     * predatory financial behaviour;
                                                                                                                     * manipulation of vulnerable users;
                                                                                                                     * evasion of legal obligations;
                                                                                                                     * harmful or prohibited goods/services.
The platform can offer redesign into a lawful and ethical version where possible.
________________


D. Founder types to support at launch
The platform should support founder types as modular profiles, not rigid archetypes. A user can belong to several founder-type categories at once.
Founder type should affect routing, benchmarking, tone, required support, and dashboard modules.
1. First-time founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * little or no entrepreneurship experience;
                                                                                                                     * may need education and structure;
                                                                                                                     * may confuse idea excitement with evidence.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * more explanation;
                                                                                                                     * stronger validation tasks;
                                                                                                                     * clearer blockers;
                                                                                                                     * low-risk route;
                                                                                                                     * mentor search;
                                                                                                                     * foundational business education.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with first-time founders at same stage and venture type.
________________


2. Repeat founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * prior venture experience;
                                                                                                                     * may have useful lessons;
                                                                                                                     * may also carry bad habits or debt.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * skip beginner content where justified;
                                                                                                                     * extract lessons from prior venture;
                                                                                                                     * identify repeated failure patterns;
                                                                                                                     * use advanced benchmarks.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with repeat founders, not only first-time founders.
________________


3. Failed founder / restart founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * previous business failed;
                                                                                                                     * may have debt, shame, resilience, or strong practical insight.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * failure analysis;
                                                                                                                     * debt-conscious restart;
                                                                                                                     * cash discipline;
                                                                                                                     * pattern prevention.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with restart founders and debt-conscious routes.
________________


4. Side-hustle founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * employed or otherwise occupied;
                                                                                                                     * limited weekly time;
                                                                                                                     * may need low-intensity validation.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * time-bounded tasks;
                                                                                                                     * low-capital path;
                                                                                                                     * schedule design;
                                                                                                                     * no premature quitting.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with other part-time founders, not full-time founders.
________________


5. Full-time founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * more time available;
                                                                                                                     * potentially higher burn rate;
                                                                                                                     * greater urgency.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * runway checks;
                                                                                                                     * execution rhythm;
                                                                                                                     * faster validation;
                                                                                                                     * spending controls.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with full-time founders with similar runway and venture type.
________________


6. Necessity entrepreneur
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * pursuing entrepreneurship due to unemployment, financial pressure, exclusion, or lack of alternatives.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * financial stabilization;
                                                                                                                     * income-first options;
                                                                                                                     * low-capital validation;
                                                                                                                     * job/income parallel route;
                                                                                                                     * strict spending blocks.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with similar runway and constraint profiles, not well-funded founders.
________________


7. Opportunity entrepreneur
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * pursuing a perceived market opportunity from a position of relative stability.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * market validation;
                                                                                                                     * business model testing;
                                                                                                                     * strategic route selection;
                                                                                                                     * opportunity pipeline.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with similar opportunity-driven founders.
________________


8. Domain expert founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * strong knowledge of a specific field;
                                                                                                                     * may lack startup or sales experience.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * commercial translation;
                                                                                                                     * external validation beyond expert assumptions;
                                                                                                                     * mentor/co-founder support if needed.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with domain experts commercializing expertise.
________________


9. Technical founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * can build product;
                                                                                                                     * may overbuild or under-validate.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * customer discovery gates;
                                                                                                                     * MVP scope limits;
                                                                                                                     * commercial translation;
                                                                                                                     * product-market evidence.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with technical founders in similar product categories.
________________


10. Non-technical founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * cannot build technical product alone;
                                                                                                                     * may need co-founder, freelancer, no-code route, or manual MVP.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * technical-gap detection;
                                                                                                                     * co-founder/advisor/freelancer path;
                                                                                                                     * no-code/manual validation;
                                                                                                                     * outsourcing safeguards.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with non-technical founders and the support they secured.
________________


11. Commercial / sales founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * strong at selling, networking, persuasion, or deal-making;
                                                                                                                     * may underbuild operations or product depth.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * delivery feasibility;
                                                                                                                     * product quality;
                                                                                                                     * operations discipline;
                                                                                                                     * claim verification.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with commercial-led founders in similar markets.
________________


12. Operations founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * strong in systems, delivery, process, quality, and execution;
                                                                                                                     * may be weaker in sales, vision, or risk-taking.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * customer acquisition;
                                                                                                                     * opportunity selection;
                                                                                                                     * positioning;
                                                                                                                     * growth rhythm.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with operations-led founders and service founders.
________________


13. Creative founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * strong originality, storytelling, design, brand, or content;
                                                                                                                     * may struggle with structure, pricing, or consistency.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * monetization logic;
                                                                                                                     * audience validation;
                                                                                                                     * operational routines;
                                                                                                                     * IP and platform dependency.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with creator/creative venture patterns.
________________


14. Impact / mission-driven founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * motivated by social, civic, environmental, or cultural purpose;
                                                                                                                     * may resist commercial language.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * theory of change;
                                                                                                                     * resource model;
                                                                                                                     * stakeholder mapping;
                                                                                                                     * impact metrics;
                                                                                                                     * funding strategy.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with impact, non-profit, hybrid, grant, and community venture routes.
________________


15. Academic / researcher / deep-tech founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * strong technical or research capability;
                                                                                                                     * weak commercial translation;
                                                                                                                     * may have IP and technology-readiness issues.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * commercialization roadmap;
                                                                                                                     * IP/professional review;
                                                                                                                     * industry mentor;
                                                                                                                     * beachhead market;
                                                                                                                     * grant/partnership routes.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare broadly at launch; exact benchmarks likely directional until enough deep-tech data exists.
________________


16. Accidental entrepreneur
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * already has customers or demand unexpectedly;
                                                                                                                     * may be overwhelmed by operations.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * operations cleanup;
                                                                                                                     * pricing;
                                                                                                                     * capacity;
                                                                                                                     * admin/tax basics;
                                                                                                                     * burnout prevention.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with early-traction operators, not idea-stage users.
________________


17. Freelancer / solo operator
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * sells personal skill or service;
                                                                                                                     * may struggle to scale beyond themselves.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * offer packaging;
                                                                                                                     * pricing;
                                                                                                                     * client pipeline;
                                                                                                                     * scheduling;
                                                                                                                     * automation;
                                                                                                                     * service boundaries;
                                                                                                                     * transition to agency/product if desired.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with service and solo-founder profiles.
________________


18. Team founder / co-founder team
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * more than one founder;
                                                                                                                     * strengths may compensate;
                                                                                                                     * conflict and role clarity become important.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * role map;
                                                                                                                     * decision rights;
                                                                                                                     * co-founder fit;
                                                                                                                     * communication norms;
                                                                                                                     * equity/legal review warnings;
                                                                                                                     * team skill coverage.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with team-based ventures where possible.
________________


19. Corporate-transition founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * leaving or considering leaving employment;
                                                                                                                     * often has domain skill and savings;
                                                                                                                     * may lack entrepreneurial network.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * transition plan;
                                                                                                                     * runway;
                                                                                                                     * external validation;
                                                                                                                     * risk gates;
                                                                                                                     * network expansion.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with professional-to-founder transition profiles.
________________


20. Low-resource founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * limited money, network, education, time, or tools;
                                                                                                                     * may still have strong motivation and insight.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * low-capital route;
                                                                                                                     * stabilization;
                                                                                                                     * free validation;
                                                                                                                     * mentor/community support;
                                                                                                                     * no-spend rules;
                                                                                                                     * careful pacing.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare with similar resource constraints, not privileged/well-funded founders.
________________


21. Overachiever / high-output founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * scores high and moves fast;
                                                                                                                     * may be genuinely ready or polished but unverified.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * overperformance verification;
                                                                                                                     * evidence gates;
                                                                                                                     * fast-track if verified;
                                                                                                                     * burnout watch.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare after verification, not only self-reported scores.
________________


22. Cautious / over-analytical founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * thoughtful, analytical, risk-aware;
                                                                                                                     * may delay action and hide behind planning.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * action-forcing tasks;
                                                                                                                     * external validation;
                                                                                                                     * decision deadlines;
                                                                                                                     * small experiments.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare based on action completion, not planning quality.
________________


23. Overconfident / under-validated founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * high confidence;
                                                                                                                     * weak evidence;
                                                                                                                     * may dismiss validation.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * strong evidence gates;
                                                                                                                     * challenge questions;
                                                                                                                     * blocker logic;
                                                                                                                     * no external-ready materials without proof.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare confidence-to-evidence ratio, not confidence alone.
________________


24. Returning founder / inactive user
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * user returns after weeks/months;
                                                                                                                     * old data may be stale.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * re-entry snapshot;
                                                                                                                     * freshness checks;
                                                                                                                     * task reset;
                                                                                                                     * updated route.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare current active state, not old profile only.
________________


25. Multi-project / portfolio founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * multiple ideas or ventures;
                                                                                                                     * risk of fragmentation.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * idea portfolio view;
                                                                                                                     * focus selection;
                                                                                                                     * opportunity scoring;
                                                                                                                     * resource allocation;
                                                                                                                     * kill/park/continue decisions.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare at venture level and founder capacity level separately.
________________


26. Neurodiverse or non-standard working-style founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * may have unusual strengths and constraints;
                                                                                                                     * may need different pacing, interface, structure, or communication.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * adaptable workflows;
                                                                                                                     * clear tasks;
                                                                                                                     * reduced ambiguity;
                                                                                                                     * flexible communication style;
                                                                                                                     * no identity assumptions;
                                                                                                                     * strengths-based routing.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * avoid unfair comparisons; use task/venture-context benchmarks, not identity-based benchmarks.
________________


27. Immigrant / cross-cultural / language-constrained founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * may have strong capability but local-language, legal, network, or cultural barriers.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * local-context guidance;
                                                                                                                     * language support;
                                                                                                                     * network-building;
                                                                                                                     * professional-review triggers;
                                                                                                                     * culturally aware messaging;
                                                                                                                     * translation of documents.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare by context and constraints where possible; avoid deficit framing.
________________


28. Socially isolated founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * limited support network;
                                                                                                                     * may rely too heavily on AI;
                                                                                                                     * may need human connection to progress.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * mentor search;
                                                                                                                     * community discovery;
                                                                                                                     * warm introductions;
                                                                                                                     * accountability rhythm;
                                                                                                                     * human-support requirements.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare support-network strength and progress after outreach.
________________


29. Regulated-sector founder
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * venture touches legal, health, finance, education, public safety, data, or professional rules.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * professional-review gates;
                                                                                                                     * claims control;
                                                                                                                     * risk register;
                                                                                                                     * compliance checklist;
                                                                                                                     * cautious external sharing.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * compare only after sector-specific risk classification.
________________


30. Unknown / hybrid founder type
Characteristics:
                                                                                                                     * user does not fit standard categories;
                                                                                                                     * multiple patterns present;
                                                                                                                     * edge case.
Platform support:
                                                                                                                     * modular Founder Operating Matrix;
                                                                                                                     * no forced archetype;
                                                                                                                     * flexible routing;
                                                                                                                     * evidence-first classification.
Benchmarking:
                                                                                                                     * use general benchmarks until profile becomes clearer.
________________


E. Founder-type overlays
The platform should allow multiple founder-type overlays at the same time.
Example:
A user may be:
                                                                                                                     * first-time founder;
                                                                                                                     * non-technical founder;
                                                                                                                     * low-resource founder;
                                                                                                                     * impact-driven founder;
                                                                                                                     * immigrant founder;
                                                                                                                     * side-hustle founder.
This should produce a combined route:
Low-capital impact validation route + language/local-context support + mentor search + no paid development + funding model clarification.
Founder types should not be labels. They should be routing inputs.
________________


F. Sector + founder-type benchmark matrix
The most useful benchmarks come from combining sector and founder type.
Examples:
Sector
	Founder type
	Useful benchmark
	Local service
	Accidental entrepreneur
	Workload, pricing, repeat customers, operations readiness.
	SaaS/AI
	Non-technical founder
	Validation before build, technical support obtained, MVP route.
	Deep-tech
	Academic founder
	Commercialization milestones, mentor support, IP clarity.
	Impact venture
	Mission-driven founder
	Funding model, stakeholder validation, impact metrics.
	E-commerce
	Low-resource founder
	Inventory risk, margin, test-before-buying behaviour.
	Consulting
	Corporate-transition founder
	First client acquisition, offer clarity, safe transition.
	Marketplace
	First-time founder
	Supply-demand sequencing and cold-start progress.
	Food/catering
	Accidental entrepreneur
	Margin, capacity, permits, repeat orders.
	Fintech
	Technical founder
	Regulatory review, trust, data security, professional input.
	Creative/media
	Creator founder
	Audience traction, platform dependency, monetization path.
	At launch, many of these will be directional. Over time, they can become stronger as data accumulates.
________________


G. Launch benchmark strategy
The platform should not wait until it has perfect benchmarks to launch. It should launch with three benchmark layers.
Layer 1 — Universal early-stage benchmarks
These apply across most sectors:
                                                                                                                     * financial runway;
                                                                                                                     * time availability;
                                                                                                                     * idea clarity;
                                                                                                                     * market evidence;
                                                                                                                     * evidence quality;
                                                                                                                     * business model clarity;
                                                                                                                     * customer/funder/payer definition;
                                                                                                                     * founder-idea fit;
                                                                                                                     * founder-market fit;
                                                                                                                     * legal/ethical risk;
                                                                                                                     * execution behaviour;
                                                                                                                     * task completion;
                                                                                                                     * external-readiness.
These can be strong at launch because they are general venture principles.
Layer 2 — Sector-family benchmarks
These apply to broad categories:
                                                                                                                     * local services;
                                                                                                                     * software/SaaS/AI;
                                                                                                                     * e-commerce;
                                                                                                                     * professional services;
                                                                                                                     * food/hospitality;
                                                                                                                     * social impact;
                                                                                                                     * education;
                                                                                                                     * health/wellness;
                                                                                                                     * deep-tech;
                                                                                                                     * marketplaces;
                                                                                                                     * manufacturing/hardware.
These may be moderate or directional at launch.
Layer 3 — Niche benchmarks
These apply to very specific categories:
