                                                                                                                     * AI agent for legal intake;
                                                                                                                     * gardening service in a specific city;
                                                                                                                     * grant-funded language education for migrants;
                                                                                                                     * local bike-shed construction business;
                                                                                                                     * robotics for eldercare;
                                                                                                                     * B2B compliance SaaS for small accountants.
These should usually start as insufficient or directional until enough comparable data exists.
4th layer — Outcome benchmarks over time
Over time, the platform should develop benchmarks based on actual outcomes:
                                                                                                                     * who started;
                                                                                                                     * who stabilized first;
                                                                                                                     * who got first customers;
                                                                                                                     * who avoided bankruptcy;
                                                                                                                     * who pivoted successfully;
                                                                                                                     * who raised funding;
                                                                                                                     * who created sustainable income;
                                                                                                                     * who stopped responsibly;
                                                                                                                     * who improved readiness.
This is what will make the platform more powerful after launch.
________________


H. Minimum viable benchmark scope at launch
At launch, Changepreneurship should benchmark at least these categories:
User/founder benchmarks
                                                                                                                     * first-time versus repeat founder;
                                                                                                                     * full-time versus side-hustle founder;
                                                                                                                     * low-resource versus financially stable founder;
                                                                                                                     * technical versus non-technical founder;
                                                                                                                     * domain expert versus outsider;
                                                                                                                     * solo founder versus team founder;
                                                                                                                     * existing traction versus idea-stage user;
                                                                                                                     * necessity versus opportunity entrepreneur;
                                                                                                                     * overconfident versus underconfident profile;
                                                                                                                     * high-output versus low-execution profile.
Venture benchmarks
                                                                                                                     * service versus product versus software versus marketplace;
                                                                                                                     * local versus online/global;
                                                                                                                     * B2B versus B2C versus B2G versus non-profit/funder-driven;
                                                                                                                     * low-capital versus capital-intensive;
                                                                                                                     * regulated versus non-regulated;
                                                                                                                     * revenue-first versus grant/funder-first;
                                                                                                                     * solo-operated versus team-dependent;
                                                                                                                     * manual/service-first versus technology-first;
                                                                                                                     * validated versus unvalidated;
                                                                                                                     * traction-stage versus idea-stage.
Risk benchmarks
                                                                                                                     * financial runway;
                                                                                                                     * market evidence;
                                                                                                                     * legal/professional-review needs;
                                                                                                                     * founder-market fit;
                                                                                                                     * operations capacity;
                                                                                                                     * evidence quality;
                                                                                                                     * spending risk;
                                                                                                                     * automation risk;
                                                                                                                     * external-sharing risk.
These categories allow useful routing even before niche benchmarks mature.
________________


I. Launch priority sectors
Although the platform should support many sectors at launch, the strongest initial product focus should be on sectors where:
                                                                                                                     * users commonly start ventures;
                                                                                                                     * validation can happen quickly;
                                                                                                                     * the platform can automate meaningful tasks;
                                                                                                                     * the risk is manageable;
                                                                                                                     * documents and workflows are reusable;
                                                                                                                     * peer benchmarks can become useful quickly;
                                                                                                                     * founders have clear next actions.
Recommended priority sectors for launch:
                                                                                                                     1. Local services and trades.
                                                                                                                     2. Professional services and consulting.
                                                                                                                     3. Software, SaaS, AI and automation.
                                                                                                                     4. E-commerce, retail and consumer products.
                                                                                                                     5. Education, training and coaching.
                                                                                                                     6. Creative, media and creator economy.
                                                                                                                     7. Social impact, non-profit and community ventures.
                                                                                                                     8. Accidental entrepreneur / existing side-hustle operations.
                                                                                                                     9. Marketplaces/platforms, with strong cold-start warnings.
                                                                                                                     10. Food/hospitality, with regulatory and margin gates.
These sectors create a strong mix of practical, digital, service, and impact users.
Sectors to support cautiously at launch
                                                                                                                     * health/wellness/care;
                                                                                                                     * fintech/insurance/investment;
                                                                                                                     * legal/compliance/legal-tech;
                                                                                                                     * real estate/housing;
                                                                                                                     * deep-tech/research commercialization;
                                                                                                                     * cybersecurity/privacy;
                                                                                                                     * energy/infrastructure;
                                                                                                                     * childcare/eldercare;
                                                                                                                     * regulated education;
                                                                                                                     * manufacturing/hardware requiring certification;
                                                                                                                     * public-sector/government procurement.
These should be supported, but with professional-review gates and directional benchmarks.
________________


J. Benchmark data maturity rules
A benchmark should only become more specific when enough data exists.
Suggested maturity levels:
Data maturity
	Benchmark type allowed
	0–20 comparable cases
	No peer benchmark; use general standards only.
	20–100 comparable cases
	Directional benchmark.
	100–500 comparable cases
	Moderate benchmark.
	500+ comparable cases
	Strong benchmark, if data quality is good.
	3+ years outcome data
	Long-term outcome benchmark possible.
	10-year data
	Bankruptcy/survival benchmark possible.
	These thresholds are not final mathematical rules, but they express the principle: do not pretend precision before data exists.
What counts as comparable?
Comparable users should match on several factors:
                                                                                                                     * sector family;
                                                                                                                     * venture type;
                                                                                                                     * business model;
                                                                                                                     * phase/stage;
                                                                                                                     * founder experience;
                                                                                                                     * resource level;
                                                                                                                     * geographic/legal context where relevant;
                                                                                                                     * capital intensity;
                                                                                                                     * regulation level;
                                                                                                                     * traction level.
Comparing a low-resource local service founder to a VC-backed SaaS founder is not useful.
________________


K. How to handle users outside supported sectors
If a user selects a sector the platform does not yet understand well, the platform should not reject them immediately.
It should say:
“This is a less common or specialist venture category. I can still help with general founder readiness, idea clarity, validation, risk mapping, and action planning, but sector-specific benchmarks will be directional until more comparable data exists.”
Then the platform should:
                                                                                                                     * ask more clarifying questions;
                                                                                                                     * classify the venture by business model and risk level;
                                                                                                                     * identify required mentors/advisors;
                                                                                                                     * use general benchmarks;
                                                                                                                     * avoid overconfident sector-specific claims;
                                                                                                                     * flag professional review if needed.
When to refuse or stop
The platform should refuse only when the direction is harmful, illegal, deceptive, exploitative, or unsafe in a way that cannot be responsibly supported.
________________


L. How sector support affects the dashboard
Sector and founder type should determine which dashboard blocks appear first.
Examples:
User/sector
	Dashboard emphasis
	Local service founder
	Scheduling, pricing, customer pipeline, operations, quoting/invoicing.
	SaaS founder
	Customer discovery, MVP scope, technical roadmap, subscription model.
	Non-technical software founder
	Co-founder/freelancer block, no-code MVP, validation before build.
	Impact founder
	Stakeholder map, theory of change, funder model, impact metrics.
	Food entrepreneur
	Permits, margins, capacity, food safety, local demand.
	Deep-tech founder
	IP, commercialization, industry mentor, grant/partner route.
	Marketplace founder
	Supply-demand sequencing, cold-start strategy, trust/liquidity.
	Accidental entrepreneur
	Operations cleanup, workload, pricing, admin, automation.
	Fintech founder
	Regulatory review, trust, data security, professional advice.
	Creative founder
	Audience, monetization, content cadence, platform dependency.
	The dashboard should feel customized from the beginning.
________________


M. Sector and founder support should affect AI execution
Different sectors need different real-world AI actions.
Examples:
Sector/founder type
	AI execution opportunities
	Local service
	Draft quotes, customer follow-up, scheduling messages, review requests.
	SaaS/AI
	Draft customer discovery, recruit testers, prepare product brief.
	E-commerce
	Compare suppliers, draft product page, test landing page, track margins.
	Impact venture
	Find grants, draft funder outreach, prepare theory of change.
	Consulting
	Draft LinkedIn outreach, package offer, prepare client proposal.
	Deep-tech
	Find industry mentors, prepare commercialization brief, draft grant application.
	Marketplace
	Recruit supply side and demand side separately.
	Food/catering
	Create menu test, collect pre-orders, calculate margin.
	Accidental entrepreneur
	Create SOPs, automate admin, classify customers, prepare price increase messages.
	Low-resource founder
	Identify free validation routes and no-cost support networks.
	This is why sector/founder classification matters beyond benchmarking.
________________


N. What the platform should not do
The platform should not:
                                                                                                                     * claim strong peer benchmarks where it has little data;
                                                                                                                     * compare users to irrelevant sectors;
                                                                                                                     * force a user into one sector if the venture is hybrid;
                                                                                                                     * treat official industry codes as sufficient for product routing;
                                                                                                                     * ignore regulation and professional-review needs;
                                                                                                                     * use VC/SaaS benchmarks for all ventures;
                                                                                                                     * benchmark non-profits as if they are normal revenue businesses;
                                                                                                                     * benchmark local services against global software startups;
                                                                                                                     * treat founder types as rigid identities;
                                                                                                                     * overlook edge-case users;
                                                                                                                     * refuse uncommon sectors unless they are unsafe or unsupported for ethical/legal reasons;
                                                                                                                     * launch with no benchmark confidence labels;
                                                                                                                     * use benchmark data in a way that compromises anonymity.
________________


O. Developer-facing requirements
The system should support:
                                                                                                                     * sector family classification;
                                                                                                                     * official sector mapping;
                                                                                                                     * venture-type overlays;
                                                                                                                     * business-model overlays;
                                                                                                                     * risk/regulation overlays;
                                                                                                                     * founder-type overlays;
                                                                                                                     * multiple classifications per user;
                                                                                                                     * benchmark confidence labels;
                                                                                                                     * data maturity thresholds;
                                                                                                                     * sector-specific question paths;
                                                                                                                     * founder-type routing;
                                                                                                                     * sector-specific dashboard blocks;
                                                                                                                     * sector-specific document templates;
                                                                                                                     * sector-specific professional-review triggers;
                                                                                                                     * sector-specific score freshness rules;
                                                                                                                     * hybrid/unknown category support;
                                                                                                                     * benchmark anonymization requirements;
                                                                                                                     * future benchmark refinement as outcome data accumulates.
________________


Final answer
At launch, Changepreneurship should support a broad range of sectors and founder types, but it should not pretend that all peer benchmarks are equally precise. The platform should support all major venture categories at the guidance and routing level, while using benchmark confidence labels such as strong, moderate, directional, general, or insufficient.
The launch sector taxonomy should include local services and trades; professional services and consulting; software, SaaS, AI and automation; e-commerce and retail; food, hospitality and catering; education and training; health, wellness and care; creative industries and media; social impact and non-profit ventures; climate and sustainability; real estate and property services; logistics and mobility; manufacturing and hardware; deep-tech and research commercialization; finance, insurance and fintech; legal and compliance; HR and recruitment; marketplaces and communities; tourism and events; agriculture and land-based ventures; government and civic innovation; wholesale/import/export; cybersecurity and privacy; franchising/licensing; and hybrid or unknown sectors.
The platform should also support many founder types at launch: first-time founders, repeat founders, failed/restart founders, side-hustle founders, full-time founders, necessity entrepreneurs, opportunity entrepreneurs, domain experts, technical founders, non-technical founders, commercial founders, operations founders, creative founders, impact founders, academic/deep-tech founders, accidental entrepreneurs, freelancers/solo operators, team founders, corporate-transition founders, low-resource founders, overachievers, cautious over-analytical founders, overconfident under-validated founders, returning founders, portfolio founders, neurodiverse or non-standard working-style founders, immigrant/cross-cultural founders, socially isolated founders, regulated-sector founders, and hybrid/unknown founder types.
The most important launch rule is that sector and founder type should drive routing, warnings, document templates, dashboard modules, AI execution options, professional-review triggers, and benchmark selection — but benchmark precision should depend on actual comparable data.
⭐ Non-negotiable: Changepreneurship should support broad diversity at launch, but it must never fake benchmark precision. A general benchmark honestly labelled as directional is better than a precise-looking benchmark built on weak or irrelevant data.
	 
 
6.4
The platform's two stated KPIs are: (1) % increase of people who become entrepreneurs, and (2) % decrease in ventures going bankrupt over 10 years. To measure these, the platform needs data from users after they leave. What data should it collect, and when — at 1 year, 3 years, 10 years?
Changepreneurship should measure long-term impact through a structured Longitudinal Venture Outcome System. The platform should not only measure whether users “started a company” or “went bankrupt.” Those are too narrow. The deeper question is whether Changepreneurship helps people make better entrepreneurial decisions over time: starting when ready, not starting when unsafe, validating before spending, avoiding unnecessary debt, building more sustainable ventures, stopping responsibly when evidence is bad, and surviving longer when they do launch.
The two stated KPIs are important:
                                                                                                                     1. Increase the percentage of users who become entrepreneurs.
                                                                                                                     2. Decrease the percentage of ventures that go bankrupt over 10 years.
However, both KPIs must be defined carefully. Otherwise the platform may accidentally optimize for the wrong behaviour.
The guiding principle is:
Changepreneurship should measure not only how many users start ventures, but whether they start responsibly, survive sustainably, avoid preventable harm, and make better decisions over time.
1. Why these KPIs need careful definitions
The first KPI — increasing the number of people who become entrepreneurs — can be dangerous if interpreted too simplistically. If the platform only tries to maximize venture starts, it may push people into entrepreneurship too early. That would contradict the mission.
The second KPI — reducing bankruptcies over 10 years — is also complex. Bankruptcy is only one visible failure outcome. Many ventures fail without formal bankruptcy. Some close responsibly. Some pivot. Some remain side hustles. Some become non-profits. Some never launch because the user wisely discovers that the idea is not viable. Some users stabilize their life first and start later. Some stop and are better off because they avoided debt.
Therefore, the platform should distinguish between:
                                                                                                                     * starting recklessly;
                                                                                                                     * starting responsibly;
                                                                                                                     * delaying responsibly;
                                                                                                                     * pausing;
                                                                                                                     * pivoting;
                                                                                                                     * closing without debt;
                                                                                                                     * closing with debt;
                                                                                                                     * formal bankruptcy;
                                                                                                                     * personal financial harm;
                                                                                                                     * sustainable operation;
                                                                                                                     * meaningful self-employment;
                                                                                                                     * non-profit or impact activity;
                                                                                                                     * improved employability or income even without a venture.
The measurement system must reward good decisions, not just venture creation.
2. Define “become an entrepreneur” precisely
The platform needs levels of entrepreneurial activation. A user should not be counted as an entrepreneur only because they had an idea or completed a questionnaire.
Recommended activation levels:
Level
	Name
	Meaning
	Counted as entrepreneur KPI?
	Level 0
	Curious / exploring
	User is exploring entrepreneurship.
	No.
	Level 1
	Idea owner
	User has a defined venture idea.
	No, but important pipeline metric.
	Level 2
	Active validator
	User is testing with real people.
	Not full entrepreneur yet, but strong progress.
	Level 3
	Pre-venture operator
	User is performing venture tasks, outreach, pilots, or pre-sales.
	Partial entrepreneurial activation.
	Level 4
	First external commitment
	User has customer, funder, partner, pilot, pre-order, donation, or serious external commitment.
	Yes, early entrepreneur.
	Level 5
	Registered or formally launched
	User registers business/non-profit or publicly launches.
	Yes.
	Level 6
	Revenue / funding / active delivery
	User earns money, receives funding, runs pilots, or delivers services.
	Yes, stronger evidence.
	Level 7
	Sustained operation
	Venture remains active for 12+ months with meaningful activity.
	Yes, high-quality entrepreneurship outcome.
	Level 8
	Sustainable venture
	Venture supports income, impact, growth, or durable operation.
	Yes, highest-quality outcome.
	The platform should report the KPI in layers:
                                                                                                                     * percentage who moved from curiosity to active validation;
                                                                                                                     * percentage who moved from validation to first external commitment;
                                                                                                                     * percentage who formally launched;
                                                                                                                     * percentage who generated revenue/funding;
                                                                                                                     * percentage who survived 1, 3, 5, and 10 years;
                                                                                                                     * percentage who became sustainably self-employed or venture-active.
This prevents shallow measurement.
3. Define “bankruptcy” and failure more carefully
The platform should track formal bankruptcy, but it should also track broader failure and harm outcomes.
Recommended outcome categories:
Outcome
	Meaning
	How to interpret
	Active and healthy
	Venture is operating with acceptable risk.
	Positive outcome.
	Active but strained
	Venture continues but has financial/operational/personal stress.
	Warning outcome.
	Paused responsibly
	User paused to stabilize or gather evidence.
	Not failure; may be good decision.
	Pivoted
	User changed idea, model, segment, or offer based on evidence.
	Often positive if evidence-based.
	Closed responsibly
	Venture stopped without major debt or harm.
	Not bankruptcy; may be success of risk control.
	Closed with loss
	Venture stopped with financial loss but not formal bankruptcy.
	Harm outcome.
	Personal debt increased
	User accumulated debt due to venture.
	Serious harm metric.
	Formal bankruptcy / insolvency
	Legal bankruptcy or insolvency event.
	Core failure KPI.
	Founder burnout / health collapse
	Venture harmed founder sustainability.
	Serious harm metric.
	Legal/regulatory problem
	Venture created legal/compliance issue.
	Serious failure/risk metric.
	Abandoned without learning
	User stopped without documented learning.
	Weak outcome.
	Did not start because unsafe
	Platform prevented unsafe action.
	Potentially positive harm-prevention outcome.
	Changepreneurship should not define every closure as failure. A responsible stop can be a success if it prevented debt, burnout, or legal risk.
4. Primary long-term KPIs
The two stated KPIs should be expanded into a broader measurement structure.
KPI 1 — Entrepreneurship activation rate
Measures how many users move from interest to meaningful entrepreneurial action.
Possible formula:
Entrepreneurship Activation Rate = users who reach Level 4 or higher / eligible users who completed enough assessment to receive a route
This should be reported by:
                                                                                                                     * all users;
                                                                                                                     * sector;
                                                                                                                     * founder type;
                                                                                                                     * resource level;
                                                                                                                     * country/region where relevant;
                                                                                                                     * phase completed;
                                                                                                                     * readiness state;
                                                                                                                     * business model;
                                                                                                                     * use of AI execution;
                                                                                                                     * mentor/co-founder support;
                                                                                                                     * pay tier.
KPI 2 — 10-year venture survival / bankruptcy reduction
Measures whether ventures formed through Changepreneurship survive longer and fail less harmfully.
Possible tracked outcomes:
                                                                                                                     * formal bankruptcy rate;
                                                                                                                     * closure with debt rate;
                                                                                                                     * closure without debt rate;
                                                                                                                     * survival at 1 year;
                                                                                                                     * survival at 3 years;
                                                                                                                     * survival at 5 years;
                                                                                                                     * survival at 10 years;
                                                                                                                     * sustained revenue/funding rate;
                                                                                                                     * founder income support rate;
                                                                                                                     * founder burnout/harm rate;
                                                                                                                     * responsible stop rate.
The platform should compare against relevant benchmarks, but only when comparisons are fair and properly contextualized.
KPI 3 — Prevented harm rate
This should be added as a third internal KPI.
Changepreneurship’s mission is not only to create more entrepreneurs. It is also to prevent avoidable harm.
Examples of prevented harm:
                                                                                                                     * user did not quit job prematurely;
                                                                                                                     * user did not take unsafe loan;
                                                                                                                     * user did not spend on product development before validation;
                                                                                                                     * user avoided inventory purchase after weak evidence;
                                                                                                                     * user sought legal review before launch;
                                                                                                                     * user pivoted before costly build;
                                                                                                                     * user stabilized income before starting;
                                                                                                                     * user closed idea responsibly before debt.
Possible formula:
Prevented Harm Rate = users who avoided a flagged high-risk action and completed a safer alternative / users who triggered a serious risk warning
This matters because some of the platform’s best outcomes may be invisible if measured only by venture starts.
KPI 4 — Responsible start rate
Measures not only whether users started, but whether they started with enough evidence and safety.
Possible conditions for responsible start:
                                                                                                                     * minimum runway or safe low-capital route;
                                                                                                                     * clear idea;
                                                                                                                     * defined target user/customer/funder;
                                                                                                                     * external evidence collected;
                                                                                                                     * business/funding model defined;
                                                                                                                     * legal/professional risks addressed;
                                                                                                                     * first offer or pilot scoped;
                                                                                                                     * no unresolved hard blockers;
                                                                                                                     * next 90-day plan exists.
This KPI is crucial because Changepreneurship should not increase entrepreneurship by increasing reckless starts.
KPI 5 — Evidence-based decision rate
Measures whether users make major decisions based on evidence rather than belief.
Examples:
                                                                                                                     * validation before product development;
                                                                                                                     * pricing tested before public launch;
                                                                                                                     * customer discovery before pitch deck;
                                                                                                                     * mentor/professional review before regulated launch;
                                                                                                                     * pivot after negative evidence;
                                                                                                                     * stop after repeated failed validation.
KPI 6 — Time-to-meaningful-progress
Measures how quickly the platform helps users reach useful milestones.
Examples:
                                                                                                                     * time to clarified idea;
                                                                                                                     * time to first customer conversation;
                                                                                                                     * time to first external commitment;
                                                                                                                     * time to first revenue/funding;
                                                                                                                     * time to first mentor conversation;
                                                                                                                     * time to first responsible stop/pivot;
                                                                                                                     * time to launch.
This helps measure whether the platform reduces wasted time.
________________


5. Data to collect at baseline
Long-term measurement starts before the user leaves. The platform needs a baseline to compare outcomes later.
A. User baseline data
Collect during onboarding and Self-Discovery:
                                                                                                                     * age range;
                                                                                                                     * country/region;
                                                                                                                     * employment status;
                                                                                                                     * income stability category;
                                                                                                                     * available weekly time;
                                                                                                                     * financial runway category;
                                                                                                                     * debt pressure category;
                                                                                                                     * education/training background;
                                                                                                                     * professional experience;
                                                                                                                     * entrepreneurship experience;
                                                                                                                     * skill profile;
                                                                                                                     * support network strength;
                                                                                                                     * risk tolerance;
                                                                                                                     * motivation type;
                                                                                                                     * founder type overlays;
                                                                                                                     * personal constraints;
                                                                                                                     * readiness state;
                                                                                                                     * user goals.
Sensitive data should be collected carefully and only when needed. The platform should use categories where possible rather than unnecessary exact personal details.
B. Venture baseline data
Collect when the user starts or defines a venture:
                                                                                                                     * venture name or working title;
                                                                                                                     * sector family;
                                                                                                                     * official sector mapping where useful;
                                                                                                                     * business model type;
                                                                                                                     * venture type: business, non-profit, social enterprise, hybrid, project, side hustle;
                                                                                                                     * B2B/B2C/B2G/non-profit/funder-driven model;
                                                                                                                     * local/online/global scope;
                                                                                                                     * capital intensity;
                                                                                                                     * regulated-sector status;
                                                                                                                     * stage at entry;
                                                                                                                     * existing traction;
                                                                                                                     * existing revenue/funding;
                                                                                                                     * team/co-founder status;
                                                                                                                     * mentor/advisor status;
                                                                                                                     * market evidence level;
                                                                                                                     * legal/professional-review needs;
                                                                                                                     * starting documents/resources.
C. Behaviour baseline data
Collect from platform behaviour:
                                                                                                                     * phases started;
                                                                                                                     * phases completed;
                                                                                                                     * questions answered;
                                                                                                                     * tasks completed;
                                                                                                                     * tasks skipped;
                                                                                                                     * evidence submitted;
                                                                                                                     * AI actions used;
                                                                                                                     * documents generated;
                                                                                                                     * external outputs shared;
                                                                                                                     * outreach sent;
                                                                                                                     * replies received;
                                                                                                                     * mentor/co-founder/funder/customer contacts made;
                                                                                                                     * warnings triggered;
                                                                                                                     * blockers resolved;
                                                                                                                     * automation credits used;
                                                                                                                     * dashboard activity.
This behavioural data is often more reliable than self-reported intention.
________________


6. Data to collect when the user leaves or becomes inactive
Users may “leave” in different ways. They may complete all phases, stop using the platform, pause because they are not ready, switch to another tool, launch the venture, or abandon the idea.
The platform should collect an exit snapshot whenever one of these happens:
                                                                                                                     * user completes all 7 phases;
                                                                                                                     * user cancels subscription;
                                                                                                                     * user has been inactive for a defined period;
                                                                                                                     * user manually archives venture;
                                                                                                                     * user marks venture launched;
                                                                                                                     * user marks venture stopped;
                                                                                                                     * user exports final package;
                                                                                                                     * user moves into ongoing operating mode.
Exit snapshot data
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * last active phase;
                                                                                                                     * completion state;
                                                                                                                     * current venture status;
                                                                                                                     * current readiness state;
                                                                                                                     * current blockers;
                                                                                                                     * current evidence level;
                                                                                                                     * documents generated;
                                                                                                                     * external materials shared;
                                                                                                                     * tasks completed;
                                                                                                                     * tasks unresolved;
                                                                                                                     * AI actions performed;
                                                                                                                     * contacts/outreach completed;
                                                                                                                     * first customer/funder/partner/mentor/co-founder status;
                                                                                                                     * revenue/funding status;
                                                                                                                     * personal financial risk status;
                                                                                                                     * venture financial risk status;
                                                                                                                     * next recommended mode;
                                                                                                                     * reason for leaving if user provides it;
                                                                                                                     * permission to follow up;
                                                                                                                     * preferred follow-up channel.
The exit snapshot becomes the starting point for long-term outcome tracking.
________________


7. Follow-up schedule
The platform should collect outcome data on a structured timeline.
Recommended follow-up schedule:
Time after exit / launch / last active state
	Purpose
	30 days
	Check immediate next action, whether user continued, and whether blockers changed.
	90 days
	Measure early progress, task completion, first evidence, and whether route remained useful.
	6 months
	Check launch status, revenue/funding, customer activity, debt, and major pivots.
	1 year
	Measure entrepreneurial activation, venture status, revenue/funding, survival, and harm prevention.
	2 years
	Track survival, growth, closure, pivots, and founder wellbeing.
	3 years
	Major outcome checkpoint: sustainability, employment/income effect, closure reasons, debt, bankruptcy.
	5 years
	Long-term survival and quality checkpoint.
	7 years
	Mid-long-term persistence and outcome checkpoint.
	10 years
	Final core KPI checkpoint: survival, bankruptcy, closure type, founder outcome.
	The questionnaire specifically mentions 1 year, 3 years, and 10 years. These should be major checkpoints, but shorter intervals are needed to understand the path.
Why shorter follow-ups matter
If the platform only checks at 10 years, it cannot learn why a venture succeeded or failed. It needs earlier data to understand:
                                                                                                                     * whether users acted on recommendations;
                                                                                                                     * when they stopped;
                                                                                                                     * when they pivoted;
                                                                                                                     * when debt appeared;
                                                                                                                     * when revenue started;
                                                                                                                     * when legal issues appeared;
                                                                                                                     * when burnout appeared;
                                                                                                                     * which platform interventions helped.
The 10-year KPI requires 10 years of data, but the system must learn every month and every year along the way.
________________


8. What to collect at 30 days
Purpose: confirm immediate continuation and early friction.
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * did the user continue after leaving/logging out/exporting;
                                                                                                                     * did they complete the recommended next action;
                                                                                                                     * did they contact anyone;
                                                                                                                     * did they gather evidence;
                                                                                                                     * did any blocker change;
                                                                                                                     * did they spend money;
                                                                                                                     * did they avoid a flagged risky action;
                                                                                                                     * did they need help;
                                                                                                                     * did the platform recommendation still make sense;
                                                                                                                     * did they use exported documents;
                                                                                                                     * did they share anything externally;
                                                                                                                     * did anyone respond;
                                                                                                                     * did they feel more or less confident;
                                                                                                                     * did they want to resume platform use.
This is especially useful for measuring whether the platform creates momentum.
________________


9. What to collect at 90 days
Purpose: measure whether the user translated the platform route into real-world action.
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * venture status: active, paused, pivoted, stopped, launched;
                                                                                                                     * number of customer/stakeholder conversations;
                                                                                                                     * first sales or funding attempts;
                                                                                                                     * first revenue/funding if any;
                                                                                                                     * mentor/advisor/co-founder progress;
                                                                                                                     * documents used externally;
                                                                                                                     * customer/funder/partner responses;
                                                                                                                     * money spent;
                                                                                                                     * debt taken;
                                                                                                                     * runway change;
                                                                                                                     * time spent;
                                                                                                                     * major obstacles;
                                                                                                                     * whether they followed the recommended route;
                                                                                                                     * whether they ignored any blockers;
                                                                                                                     * whether AI-executed actions were useful;
                                                                                                                     * whether they need re-entry support.
This measures early implementation quality.
________________


10. What to collect at 6 months
Purpose: measure early venture formation and risk outcomes.
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * is the venture still active;
                                                                                                                     * has it launched formally;
                                                                                                                     * is it registered or legally formed if relevant;
                                                                                                                     * has it generated revenue/funding;
                                                                                                                     * number of paying customers, users, donors, funders, pilots, or partners;
                                                                                                                     * monthly revenue/funding range;
                                                                                                                     * monthly costs range;
                                                                                                                     * profit/loss category;
                                                                                                                     * personal runway;
                                                                                                                     * debt increase or decrease;
                                                                                                                     * co-founder/team status;
                                                                                                                     * mentor/advisor status;
                                                                                                                     * operational workload;
                                                                                                                     * burnout/stress level category;
                                                                                                                     * major pivot or business model change;
                                                                                                                     * legal/professional issues;
                                                                                                                     * customer satisfaction or retention signals;
                                                                                                                     * whether the venture is still aligned with the original plan.
This is the first meaningful venture-health checkpoint.
________________


11. What to collect at 1 year
The 1-year follow-up is a major checkpoint.
A. Entrepreneurship activation data
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * did the user start a venture;
                                                                                                                     * what type of venture;
                                                                                                                     * formal registration status;
                                                                                                                     * public launch status;
                                                                                                                     * first revenue/funding status;
                                                                                                                     * active customers/users/beneficiaries/donors/partners;
                                                                                                                     * whether the venture is full-time, part-time, side-hustle, non-profit, project-based, or paused;
                                                                                                                     * whether the user considers themselves entrepreneurially active;
                                                                                                                     * whether the venture replaced or supplemented income.
B. Venture performance data
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * current status: active, paused, pivoted, closed, sold, merged, archived;
                                                                                                                     * revenue/funding range;
                                                                                                                     * profit/loss category;
                                                                                                                     * customer/user count range;
                                                                                                                     * repeat customer/user behaviour;
                                                                                                                     * funding/grant/investment status;
                                                                                                                     * pilot/project outcomes;
                                                                                                                     * main traction source;
                                                                                                                     * main operating challenge;
                                                                                                                     * founder workload;
                                                                                                                     * team/co-founder status;
                                                                                                                     * use of mentors/advisors;
                                                                                                                     * tools/systems used.
C. Financial and harm data
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * money invested by founder;
                                                                                                                     * debt taken for venture;
                                                                                                                     * debt still outstanding;
                                                                                                                     * personal financial stress category;
                                                                                                                     * whether user quit employment;
                                                                                                                     * whether quitting was planned or premature;
                                                                                                                     * whether platform warnings prevented spending or debt;
                                                                                                                     * whether financial situation improved, stayed same, or worsened;
                                                                                                                     * whether venture caused serious personal financial harm.
D. Decision-quality data
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * did the user validate before building;
                                                                                                                     * did they follow platform route;
                                                                                                                     * did they use documents externally;
                                                                                                                     * did they pivot based on evidence;
                                                                                                                     * did they stop responsibly;
                                                                                                                     * did they seek professional review when advised;
                                                                                                                     * did they obtain mentor/co-founder support;
                                                                                                                     * which platform intervention was most useful;
                                                                                                                     * which recommendation was ignored and why.
E. User outcome data
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * confidence change;
                                                                                                                     * skill improvement;
                                                                                                                     * income change;
                                                                                                                     * employment status change;
                                                                                                                     * wellbeing/stress category;
                                                                                                                     * perceived usefulness of platform;
                                                                                                                     * whether they would recommend it;
                                                                                                                     * whether they would use it again;
                                                                                                                     * whether they avoided a mistake because of it.
At 1 year, the platform should be able to measure whether the user became entrepreneurially active and whether early harm was prevented.
________________


12. What to collect at 3 years
The 3-year checkpoint should measure sustainability.
A. Venture survival data
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * is the venture still active;
                                                                                                                     * if not, when did it stop;
                                                                                                                     * why did it stop;
                                                                                                                     * did it close responsibly or under distress;
                                                                                                                     * did it pivot into another venture;
                                                                                                                     * did it merge, sell, or evolve;
                                                                                                                     * current legal/registration status;
                                                                                                                     * whether the venture has employees, contractors, volunteers, or partners;
                                                                                                                     * whether operations are stable.
B. Financial sustainability data
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * annual revenue/funding range;
                                                                                                                     * profitability/surplus category;
                                                                                                                     * founder income from venture;
                                                                                                                     * debt level;
                                                                                                                     * outstanding obligations;
                                                                                                                     * investment raised;
                                                                                                                     * grants/donations received;
                                                                                                                     * cash-flow stability;
                                                                                                                     * whether founder’s personal financial situation improved or worsened.
C. Failure and harm data
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * formal bankruptcy/insolvency event;
                                                                                                                     * closure with unpaid debt;
                                                                                                                     * supplier/customer disputes;
                                                                                                                     * legal/regulatory issues;
                                                                                                                     * severe burnout or health impact;
                                                                                                                     * relationship/family impact category;
                                                                                                                     * reputational harm;
                                                                                                                     * lessons learned;
                                                                                                                     * whether earlier platform blockers were relevant.
D. Longitudinal learning data
Collect:
                                                                                                                     * which early scores predicted outcome;
                                                                                                                     * which warnings were accurate;
                                                                                                                     * which warnings were false positives;
                                                                                                                     * which founder types survived best in which sectors;
                                                                                                                     * which interventions improved survival;
                                                                                                                     * which AI-executed tasks correlated with progress;
                                                                                                                     * which documents were actually used;
                                                                                                                     * which marketplace integrations led to useful outcomes;
