                                                                                                                                 * user consent controls;
                                                                                                                                 * professional-review warnings.
Users should not have to pay extra to understand why the platform is preventing harm.
________________


22. Pricing examples
Example 1 — Basic idea clarification
User asks AI to help clarify a rough idea.
                                                                                                                                 * Actual cost: €0.40
                                                                                                                                 * Billed price: €1.00
                                                                                                                                 * User sees: “This clarification will use 10 credits.”
Example 2 — Market research scan
AI runs competitor and market scan.
                                                                                                                                 * Actual cost: €4.00
                                                                                                                                 * Billed price: €10.00
                                                                                                                                 * User sees: “Estimated cost: 90–110 credits. Maximum: 110 credits.”
Example 3 — Customer outreach preparation
AI identifies contacts, drafts 20 messages, prepares interview guide.
                                                                                                                                 * Actual cost: €3.20
                                                                                                                                 * Billed price: €8.00
                                                                                                                                 * User sees: “This will use 80 credits. Sending requires separate approval.”
Example 4 — Full investor deck regeneration
AI regenerates pitch deck after strategy change.
                                                                                                                                 * Actual cost: €8.00
                                                                                                                                 * Billed price: €20.00
                                                                                                                                 * User sees: “Regenerate full deck for 200 credits, or update only affected slides for 60 credits.”
Example 5 — Weekly monitoring
AI monitors grants, competitor updates, stale documents, and outreach replies.
                                                                                                                                 * Actual cost: variable
                                                                                                                                 * Billed price: actual cost × 2.5
                                                                                                                                 * User sets cap: “Maximum 150 credits per month for monitoring.”
________________


23. Product wording for pricing
The platform should explain pricing simply.
Possible wording:
“Changepreneurship uses resource-metered pricing. You pay for the work the platform performs: AI reasoning, document generation, research, automations, integrations, and approved external actions. Before any meaningful paid action, you see the estimated cost and can choose a manual or lower-cost alternative.”
More concise:
“You only pay when the platform performs paid work. Every paid action shows its cost before execution.”
For the markup, internal wording:
“Billed price is calculated as actual resource cost × 2.5.”
External wording can be simpler unless transparency is desired:
“Usage prices include platform resource costs plus a service margin.”
________________


24. Model selection and cost-optimization principle
Changepreneurship should always use the most optimal and up-to-date AI models available for the task, but “optimal” should not automatically mean “most expensive.” The correct model is the one that delivers the required quality, safety, reliability, and reasoning depth at the lowest responsible cost.
The platform should therefore use a cost-optimized model-routing system.
The guiding principle is:
Use the cheapest capable method first, escalate only when quality, risk, complexity, or verification requires it, and never bill the customer for costs Changepreneurship did not incur.
This is essential to the fairness of the resource-metered pricing model. If Changepreneurship can complete a task for free, through open-source models, through cached results, through deterministic rules, or through a lower-cost model without quality loss, then the user should benefit from that saving. The platform should not artificially route simple work through expensive models merely to increase billed usage.
A. Cost-routing hierarchy
The AI should perform work through the cheapest responsible route first.
Recommended hierarchy:
Cost tier
	Meaning
	When to use
	Free
	No direct marginal model or external-resource cost.
	Fixed rules, cached answers, simple calculations, stored records, deterministic checks, already-available internal data.
	Open-source
	Self-hosted or low-cost open model where direct cost is minimal.
	Classification, extraction, simple drafting, tagging, summarization, internal preprocessing.
	Lowest price per 1M tokens
	Cheapest paid capable model.
	Low-risk routine generation, simple explanation, basic rewriting, ordinary summaries.
	Low price per 1M tokens
	Low-cost paid model with stronger capability.
	Moderate synthesis, dashboard copy, simple reasoning, task decomposition.
	Medium price per 1M tokens
	Balanced model for higher-quality reasoning.
	Founder profile synthesis, routing explanation, document sections, nuanced analysis.
	High price per 1M tokens
	Strong model for complex or higher-risk work.
	Contradiction detection, important recommendations, external-ready drafts, complex strategy.
	Top price per 1M tokens
	Most capable available model.
	High-stakes reasoning, final review, safety-critical checks, complex investor/bank/funder/professional materials, edge cases.
	The platform should not treat model cost as the only decision factor. It should consider capability, benchmark performance, release recency, context length, reliability, latency, safety behaviour, privacy/security requirements, language quality, tool-use ability, and suitability for the specific task.
B. Higher-tier models as reviewers and verifiers
Higher-tier paid models should often be used as reviewers rather than as the first worker for every task.
A practical workflow:
                                                                                                                                 1. Low-cost or open-source model performs the brunt work.
It extracts, summarizes, classifies, drafts, cleans, tags, or prepares structured data.
                                                                                                                                 2. Medium or high-tier model checks important reasoning.
It reviews coherence, contradictions, missing risks, and routing logic.
                                                                                                                                 3. Top-tier model is used only where justified.
It verifies high-stakes outputs, external-ready documents, complex edge cases, or safety-critical recommendations.
This means expensive models are not wasted on work that cheaper methods can perform reliably.
Example:
An open-source model may classify 100 customer replies into themes. A medium-tier model may summarize the patterns. A high-tier model may review whether the evidence justifies changing the Market Evidence status. A top-tier model may be used only if the result affects investor, bank, legal, or launch readiness.
C. “Actual cost” must reflect the chosen route
Because the billing formula is actual cost × 2.5, the actual cost must reflect the real route used.
If the task used:
                                                                                                                                    * fixed rules only, actual model cost should be zero or near-zero;
                                                                                                                                    * cached data, the user should not be charged again for expensive recomputation unless new work is performed;
                                                                                                                                    * open-source models with negligible marginal cost, the billed amount should reflect that lower cost;
                                                                                                                                    * a cheap model plus one expensive verification call, the billed amount should reflect that combined actual cost;
                                                                                                                                    * a top-tier model because the user approved a high-stakes action, the cost should be shown transparently.
The platform must not bill as if a top-tier model was used when a cheaper model performed the work.
D. No incurred cost means no passed-on cost
A key fairness rule should be:
If Changepreneurship does not incur a direct resource cost for an action, the user should not be charged a metered resource cost for that action.
This does not mean the entire platform must be free. A base subscription, enterprise license, or included access fee may still cover general platform availability, storage, support, and product maintenance. But for metered usage, the charge must correspond to real resource consumption.
Examples:
Action
	Direct incurred cost
	Metered billing result
	Updating a status using fixed rule
	None/negligible
	Free or included.
	Showing already-generated dashboard data
	None/negligible
	Free or included.
	Reusing cached market scan
	None/new cost minimal
	Free, included, or very low cost.
	Running open-source classification locally
	Low infrastructure cost only
	Low billed cost.
	Calling paid model for deep reasoning
	Paid model cost
	Actual cost × 2.5.
	Using external paid data API
	Paid API cost
	Actual cost × 2.5, with pass-through shown if relevant.
	This creates trust and prevents the credit system from becoming a dark pattern.
E. Model capability and benchmarking
Changepreneurship should maintain an internal model capability registry.
For each model, the platform should track:
                                                                                                                                    * provider;
                                                                                                                                    * model name/version;
                                                                                                                                    * release date;
                                                                                                                                    * input token price;
                                                                                                                                    * output token price;
                                                                                                                                    * combined estimated cost per 1M input + output tokens;
                                                                                                                                    * context window;
                                                                                                                                    * supported modalities;
                                                                                                                                    * supported languages;
                                                                                                                                    * tool-use ability;
                                                                                                                                    * reasoning strength;
                                                                                                                                    * writing quality;
                                                                                                                                    * extraction/classification performance;
                                                                                                                                    * safety reliability;
                                                                                                                                    * latency;
                                                                                                                                    * privacy/security suitability;
                                                                                                                                    * benchmark scores where available;
                                                                                                                                    * internal evaluation results;
                                                                                                                                    * failure modes;
                                                                                                                                    * recommended task categories;
                                                                                                                                    * banned or restricted task categories.
The platform should not assume price alone equals capability. Higher price may often correlate with capability, especially for recent flagship models, but the platform should verify this through benchmarking and task-specific evaluation.
The better rule is:
Assume higher-priced recent models may be more capable, but prove model suitability through benchmarks, internal tests, task performance, and safety behaviour.
F. Recency matters
The platform should prefer up-to-date models where they provide better quality, safety, efficiency, or capability. A cheaper older model may be suitable for routine work, but high-stakes reasoning should consider whether newer models perform better.
Model selection should consider:
                                                                                                                                    * how recently the model was released;
                                                                                                                                    * whether a newer model improves quality or reduces cost;
                                                                                                                                    * whether a cheaper model now outperforms an older expensive model;
                                                                                                                                    * whether an open-source model has become good enough for a task;
                                                                                                                                    * whether provider pricing changed;
                                                                                                                                    * whether safety or privacy requirements changed;
                                                                                                                                    * whether benchmark performance changed.
This means model routing should be reviewed continuously, not hard-coded permanently.
G. Task-based model routing
Different tasks should use different model tiers.
Examples:
Task
	Preferred route
	Simple status update
	Fixed rule / free.
	Basic tagging/classification
	Open-source or lowest-cost model.
	Summarizing ordinary notes
	Open-source or low-cost model.
	Drafting simple internal task
	Low-cost model.
	Founder Operating Matrix synthesis
	Medium or high-tier model, depending on complexity.
	External mentor brief
	Medium-tier draft + high-tier review if needed.
	Investor deck
	Medium/high-tier generation + high/top-tier review.
	Bank memo
	High-tier reasoning + professional-review warning.
	Legal/tax/regulatory issue
	AI preparation only + professional review; no final conclusion.
	Customer outreach at scale
	Low/medium drafting + quality sampling + user approval.
	High-risk external message
	High-tier review before approval.
	Safety-critical claim
	Professional review required; AI cannot finalize.
	This avoids waste while protecting quality.
H. User-facing transparency
The user does not need to see internal model names for every tiny step, but the platform should be transparent about cost level.
Possible user-facing labels:
                                                                                                                                    * Included / no extra resource cost.
                                                                                                                                    * Low-cost AI action.
                                                                                                                                    * Standard AI action.
                                                                                                                                    * Advanced AI action.
                                                                                                                                    * High-stakes review.
                                                                                                                                    * External data/API cost included.
                                                                                                                                    * Manual alternative available.
For advanced users, there could be an expanded view showing:
                                                                                                                                    * model tier used;
                                                                                                                                    * reason for model choice;
                                                                                                                                    * estimated cost;
                                                                                                                                    * cheaper alternative;
                                                                                                                                    * quality trade-off;
                                                                                                                                    * whether higher-tier review is included.
Example:
“Recommended route: low-cost drafting plus advanced review. Estimated cost: 42 credits. Cheaper option: draft only for 18 credits, but not recommended for investor-facing material.”
I. User control over cost-quality trade-off
Users should sometimes be able to choose between cost and quality levels.
Example options:
Option
	Meaning
	Cheapest acceptable
	Use the lowest-cost capable route.
	Balanced
	Use low/medium model plus checks where needed.
	Highest quality
	Use stronger models and more review.
	Manual-first
	Generate instructions/templates but let user perform work manually.
	Review only
	User writes, AI reviews.
	However, the platform should not allow the user to choose a cheap route when the output is high-stakes and unsafe without stronger review.
Example:
“A cheaper draft-only route is available, but this document cannot be marked bank-ready without advanced review and professional-review warnings.”
J. Cost optimization must not reduce safety
The platform should not use cheap models where failure could harm the user.
Do not use the cheapest model for:
                                                                                                                                    * high-stakes financial decisions;
                                                                                                                                    * legal/professional-review routing;
                                                                                                                                    * external-ready investor/bank/funder materials;
                                                                                                                                    * regulated claims;
                                                                                                                                    * safety-critical products;
                                                                                                                                    * sensitive personal readiness conclusions;
                                                                                                                                    * actions that contact high-value or sensitive people;
                                                                                                                                    * anything that could create legal, reputational, or financial harm.
Cost minimization is important, but not at the expense of truth, safety, or user trust.
K. Internal optimization and margin fairness
The 2.5 multiplier creates a margin on the actual cost. If Changepreneurship reduces actual cost through better model routing, open-source adoption, caching, or deterministic rules, that saving should flow partly to the user through lower billed usage.
This keeps incentives aligned:
                                                                                                                                    * the company benefits from efficient infrastructure;
                                                                                                                                    * users benefit from lower costs;
                                                                                                                                    * the platform remains motivated to optimize;
                                                                                                                                    * trust increases because pricing reflects real resource use.
The platform should not keep charging old high rates after cheaper equally capable infrastructure becomes available, unless a separate subscription or service fee clearly covers non-metered value.
L. Developer-facing requirements for model routing
The system should support:
                                                                                                                                    * model registry;
                                                                                                                                    * provider price tracking;
                                                                                                                                    * release-date tracking;
                                                                                                                                    * benchmark tracking;
                                                                                                                                    * internal evaluation scores;
                                                                                                                                    * task-to-model routing rules;
                                                                                                                                    * dynamic model selection;
                                                                                                                                    * fallback models;
                                                                                                                                    * open-source model integration;
                                                                                                                                    * self-hosted model cost accounting;
                                                                                                                                    * cached result detection;
                                                                                                                                    * free/deterministic route detection;
                                                                                                                                    * cost estimation before execution;
                                                                                                                                    * actual model cost logging;
                                                                                                                                    * input and output token logging where appropriate;
                                                                                                                                    * user-facing cost tier labels;
                                                                                                                                    * higher-tier review workflows;
                                                                                                                                    * safety-based escalation rules;
                                                                                                                                    * user cost-quality preferences;
                                                                                                                                    * model performance monitoring;
                                                                                                                                    * automatic routing updates when pricing or capability changes;
                                                                                                                                    * audit trail for model choice in high-impact actions.
M. What the platform should not do
The platform should not:
                                                                                                                                    * always use the most expensive model by default;
                                                                                                                                    * always use the cheapest model when quality or safety requires more;
                                                                                                                                    * bill users for model costs that were not incurred;
                                                                                                                                    * hide that a cheaper route exists;
                                                                                                                                    * route paid work through expensive models merely to increase revenue;
                                                                                                                                    * assume price alone proves quality;
                                                                                                                                    * ignore open-source models that can reduce user cost;
                                                                                                                                    * keep old routing after better cheaper models become available;
                                                                                                                                    * use low-quality models for high-stakes outputs;
                                                                                                                                    * let user-selected cheap mode bypass professional-review gates;
                                                                                                                                    * present low-cost AI outputs as externally verified when they are not.
________________


25. Developer-facing requirements
The system should support:
                                                                                                                                    * action-level cost estimation;
                                                                                                                                    * actual direct cost tracking;
                                                                                                                                    * multiplier-based billing;
                                                                                                                                    * credit wallet;
                                                                                                                                    * credit packages;
                                                                                                                                    * free allowances;
                                                                                                                                    * subscription allowances if used;
                                                                                                                                    * monthly caps;
                                                                                                                                    * per-action approval thresholds;
                                                                                                                                    * variable cost caps;
                                                                                                                                    * pass-through fee separation;
                                                                                                                                    * usage ledger;
                                                                                                                                    * billing audit trail;
                                                                                                                                    * refund logic;
                                                                                                                                    * failed-action handling;
                                                                                                                                    * manual alternatives;
                                                                                                                                    * low-cost mode;
                                                                                                                                    * enterprise pooled credits;
                                                                                                                                    * institutional reporting;
                                                                                                                                    * cost by category;
                                                                                                                                    * cost warnings before execution;
                                                                                                                                    * recurring automation caps;
                                                                                                                                    * user-facing billing explanations;
                                                                                                                                    * admin tools for adjusting/refunding credits.
25. What the platform should not do
The platform should not:
                                                                                                                                    * offer unlimited high-cost AI usage under a flat subscription;
                                                                                                                                    * hide usage costs until after the action;
                                                                                                                                    * run paid automations without approval or cap;
                                                                                                                                    * recommend paid actions where manual action is clearly better;
                                                                                                                                    * push high-cost actions to financially vulnerable users;
                                                                                                                                    * charge for platform failures without remedy;
                                                                                                                                    * make pricing so complex that users cannot predict spend;
                                                                                                                                    * treat credits as a dark pattern;
                                                                                                                                    * hide cheaper alternatives;
                                                                                                                                    * use referral fees to bias recommendations;
                                                                                                                                    * monetize desperation;
                                                                                                                                    * allow AI to spend money on behalf of the user without consent;
                                                                                                                                    * confuse actual platform cost, billed price, and external pass-through cost.
________________


Final answer
Changepreneurship should make money primarily through resource-metered usage pricing. The platform charges for the real work it performs and the real resources it consumes: AI reasoning, document generation, market research, data processing, integrations, outreach preparation, automation, external-platform actions, monitoring, storage, and execution. The billing rule should be: actual cost × 2.5 = billed price. This creates a clear margin while keeping the model connected to real resource consumption.
The user-facing model should use a credit wallet, preferably called Venture Credits. Users can receive free credits, buy credit bundles, or receive credits through a subscription or institution. Before any meaningful paid action, the platform should show the estimated cost, expected benefit, manual alternative, lower-cost alternative, and approval requirement. Users should have spending caps, low-cost mode, manual-first mode, and full usage history. Heavy usage is paid; basic access, safety explanations, consent controls, and manual alternatives should remain accessible.
⭐ Non-negotiable: pricing must be transparent, metered, and consent-based. The platform may charge for real work performed, but it must never become a hidden meter, a pay-to-understand system, or a machine that profits from reckless founder behaviour.
	 
 
8.2
What is completely free?
Completely free should include access, orientation, transparency, safety explanations, basic manual tools, limited low-cost guidance, and the user’s own data controls — but not unlimited resource-consuming AI work.
The guiding principle should be:
Anything necessary for trust, safety, consent, basic understanding, and user control should be free. Anything that consumes meaningful resources, automates work, generates major outputs, or performs external actions can be metered.
Free should include:
                                                                                                                                    * public website and explanation of the platform
                                                                                                                                    * account creation and login
                                                                                                                                    * pricing visibility and cost estimates
                                                                                                                                    * consent, permission, and privacy controls
                                                                                                                                    * basic onboarding and orientation
                                                                                                                                    * limited founder/venture assessment preview
                                                                                                                                    * basic safety explanations
                                                                                                                                    * manual alternatives to paid AI actions
                                                                                                                                    * basic dashboard shell
                                                                                                                                    * viewing already-created outputs
                                                                                                                                    * simple manual task tracking
                                                                                                                                    * basic status labels and blockers
                                                                                                                                    * user data correction
                                                                                                                                    * basic export of user-owned data
                                                                                                                                    * support for billing, privacy, account, or safety issues
Free with a limited allowance may include:
                                                                                                                                    * a small number of AI chat messages
                                                                                                                                    * a basic founder readiness preview
                                                                                                                                    * one simple idea clarification
                                                                                                                                    * one basic task-plan draft
                                                                                                                                    * one limited document preview
                                                                                                                                    * a small amount of Venture Credits
                                                                                                                                    * limited saved records, file uploads, and storage
Not completely free:
                                                                                                                                    * full Founder Operating Matrix
                                                                                                                                    * deep readiness analysis
                                                                                                                                    * full business plan
                                                                                                                                    * pitch deck
                                                                                                                                    * bank memo
                                                                                                                                    * grant proposal
                                                                                                                                    * investor memo
                                                                                                                                    * market research
                                                                                                                                    * competitor scan
                                                                                                                                    * grant/funding search
                                                                                                                                    * connected-account analysis at scale
                                                                                                                                    * email/contact/calendar scanning
                                                                                                                                    * outreach execution
                                                                                                                                    * external-platform actions
                                                                                                                                    * ongoing monitoring
                                                                                                                                    * data room
                                                                                                                                    * large file storage
                                                                                                                                    * advanced automation
The most important rule:
The free layer must build trust and protect users, not become a hidden paywall for safety, consent, pricing clarity, or control over their own data.
	 
 
8.3
What is behind the paid tier?
Changepreneurship should use a resource-metered pricing model. This means users pay when the platform actually performs meaningful work, especially when that work creates direct costs for the system or produces valuable venture-building output.
The paid layer should not feel like a vague subscription wall. It should be understandable, predictable, and tied to visible actions.
The correct answer is:
Paid or metered actions should include deep AI analysis, major document generation, external research, connected-account processing, automation, outreach, monitoring, advanced workflows, and execution support.
The guiding principle is:
The platform may charge when it performs real work, consumes significant resources, creates business assets, saves the user time, or acts on the user’s behalf.
________________


1. What “paid” should mean in Changepreneurship
Paid should mean the user receives something substantial.
Examples:
                                                                                                                                    * a deeper analysis;
                                                                                                                                    * a generated document;
                                                                                                                                    * a market scan;
                                                                                                                                    * a competitor report;
                                                                                                                                    * a grant search;
                                                                                                                                    * an outreach campaign;
                                                                                                                                    * a processed email/contact/calendar dataset;
                                                                                                                                    * an investor-ready memo;
                                                                                                                                    * a validation plan;
                                                                                                                                    * a financial-readiness report;
                                                                                                                                    * an automated workflow;
                                                                                                                                    * a monitored opportunity stream;
                                                                                                                                    * a professional-review package;
                                                                                                                                    * a venture data room.
The user should never wonder: “What did I just pay for?”
Every paid action should have:
                                                                                                                                    * clear name;
                                                                                                                                    * short explanation;
                                                                                                                                    * estimated credit cost;
                                                                                                                                    * reason why it costs money;
                                                                                                                                    * manual alternative where possible;
                                                                                                                                    * approval before execution;
                                                                                                                                    * result after completion;
                                                                                                                                    * usage history entry.
________________


2. Deep AI analysis should be paid
Deep AI analysis should usually be metered because it uses higher reasoning, larger context, and more system resources.
Paid examples:
                                                                                                                                    * full Founder Operating Matrix;
                                                                                                                                    * founder readiness analysis;
                                                                                                                                    * founder-risk analysis;
                                                                                                                                    * venture feasibility analysis;
                                                                                                                                    * business-model analysis;
                                                                                                                                    * idea-market fit analysis;
                                                                                                                                    * founder-market fit analysis;
                                                                                                                                    * financial readiness assessment;
                                                                                                                                    * operational readiness assessment;
                                                                                                                                    * contradiction detection;
                                                                                                                                    * multi-document synthesis;
                                                                                                                                    * strategic roadmap creation;
                                                                                                                                    * decision-support analysis;
                                                                                                                                    * risk-priority scoring.
A free user may receive a basic preview, but the full analysis should be paid.
Example:
Free: “Your idea appears to be in the validation stage.”
Paid: “Full venture-readiness analysis with risks, missing evidence, recommended next steps, and founder-specific execution plan.”
________________


3. Major document generation should be paid
Documents are one of the clearest paid categories because they create concrete business assets.
Paid documents may include:
                                                                                                                                    * business plan;
                                                                                                                                    * pitch deck;
                                                                                                                                    * investor memo;
                                                                                                                                    * bank memo;
                                                                                                                                    * grant proposal;
                                                                                                                                    * partner proposal;
                                                                                                                                    * validation report;
                                                                                                                                    * customer discovery report;
                                                                                                                                    * market research report;
                                                                                                                                    * competitor analysis;
                                                                                                                                    * sales script;
                                                                                                                                    * outreach sequence;
                                                                                                                                    * onboarding document;
                                                                                                                                    * SOPs;
                                                                                                                                    * financial narrative;
                                                                                                                                    * operational roadmap;
                                                                                                                                    * professional-review pack;
                                                                                                                                    * data-room package.
The user should be able to see a preview or outline for free, but the full document should consume credits.
Example:
Free: “Suggested business plan structure.”
Paid: “Generate full 25-page business plan using my venture data.”
________________


4. Market and external research should be paid
External research often consumes API usage, browsing, search time, data extraction, and reasoning. Therefore, it should normally be metered.
Paid research may include:
                                                                                                                                    * market scan;
                                                                                                                                    * competitor scan;
                                                                                                                                    * pricing research;
                                                                                                                                    * customer-segment research;
                                                                                                                                    * legal/regulatory orientation;
                                                                                                                                    * grant/funding search;
                                                                                                                                    * sector benchmark research;
                                                                                                                                    * investor research;
                                                                                                                                    * partner search;
                                                                                                                                    * supplier search;
                                                                                                                                    * technology comparison;
                                                                                                                                    * geographic opportunity scan;
                                                                                                                                    * platform opportunity search.
This is especially important if the system must retrieve fresh information from outside sources.
Example:
“Search for Dutch grants relevant to home automation, robotics, and consumer-assistance technology.”
That should be paid or consume Venture Credits because it requires external work.
________________


5. Connected-account processing should be paid
Connecting an account should be free. Processing its contents should be metered.
Paid examples:
                                                                                                                                    * scanning email history;
                                                                                                                                    * analyzing contacts;
                                                                                                                                    * extracting leads from Gmail;
                                                                                                                                    * analyzing calendar patterns;
                                                                                                                                    * reviewing Google Drive documents;
                                                                                                                                    * processing uploaded files;
                                                                                                                                    * summarizing long email threads;
                                                                                                                                    * extracting tasks from communications;
                                                                                                                                    * analyzing customer conversations;
                                                                                                                                    * creating CRM-like records;
                                                                                                                                    * detecting relevant opportunities in old correspondence.
The distinction should be very clear:
Action
	Pricing
	Connect Gmail
	Free
	Disconnect Gmail
	Free
	View connection status
	Free
	Analyze 5 selected emails
	Free or limited allowance
	Analyze 5,000 emails
	Paid / metered
	Extract leads from email history
	Paid
	Monitor new replies
	Paid / recurring
	This avoids charging for permission while still charging for actual processing.
________________


6. Automation and execution should be paid
Any action where the platform does something on behalf of the user should usually be paid.
Paid execution may include:
                                                                                                                                    * sending emails;
                                                                                                                                    * drafting outreach;
                                                                                                                                    * sending follow-ups;
                                                                                                                                    * scheduling meetings;
                                                                                                                                    * submitting forms;
                                                                                                                                    * updating CRM records;
                                                                                                                                    * creating Trello tasks;
                                                                                                                                    * creating calendar events;
                                                                                                                                    * monitoring replies;
                                                                                                                                    * classifying responses;
                                                                                                                                    * generating next actions;
                                                                                                                                    * maintaining a pipeline;
                                                                                                                                    * checking deadlines;
                                                                                                                                    * preparing recurring reports.
This is real operational support. It should not be free because it replaces human labour and creates responsibility.
However, execution should always require consent.
The system should never silently spend credits or perform external actions without clear approval.
________________


7. Outreach should be paid
Outreach is a high-value activity and should be metered carefully.
Paid outreach may include:
                                                                                                                                    * identifying target contacts;
                                                                                                                                    * writing personalized emails;
                                                                                                                                    * generating follow-up sequences;
                                                                                                                                    * sending messages;
                                                                                                                                    * tracking replies;
                                                                                                                                    * classifying responses;
                                                                                                                                    * suggesting next action;
                                                                                                                                    * preparing call notes;
                                                                                                                                    * updating a pipeline;
                                                                                                                                    * generating weekly outreach reports.
There should be different levels:
Outreach level
	Pricing
	Manual template
	Free
	One AI-written email draft
	Low-cost / credits
	Personalized sequence for 10 leads
	Paid
	Automated follow-up campaign
	Paid + consent
	Reply classification and next actions
	Paid
	Ongoing pipeline management
	Paid plan / metered
	Outreach should never become reckless automation. The user should approve external communications unless they have explicitly configured trusted rules.
________________


8. Ongoing monitoring should be paid
Monitoring creates recurring system cost. It should not be completely free.
Paid monitoring may include:
                                                                                                                                    * grant monitoring;
                                                                                                                                    * funding deadline monitoring;
                                                                                                                                    * competitor monitoring;
                                                                                                                                    * market trend monitoring;
                                                                                                                                    * policy/regulation monitoring;
                                                                                                                                    * document freshness monitoring;
                                                                                                                                    * task-progress monitoring;
                                                                                                                                    * reply monitoring;
                                                                                                                                    * lead monitoring;
                                                                                                                                    * partnership opportunity monitoring.
Monitoring should usually be billed as:
                                                                                                                                    * recurring credits;
                                                                                                                                    * monthly plan;
                                                                                                                                    * pay-per-check;
                                                                                                                                    * capped usage.
Example:
“Check weekly for Dutch subsidies related to robotics, automation, and consumer support.”
That should be paid because it creates recurring work.
________________


9. Advanced workflows should be paid
Basic task management can be free. Intelligent workflows should be paid.
Paid workflow features may include:
                                                                                                                                    * AI task decomposition;
                                                                                                                                    * automatic prioritization;
                                                                                                                                    * blocker detection;
                                                                                                                                    * dependency mapping;
                                                                                                                                    * founder-specific planning;
                                                                                                                                    * multi-step project workflows;
                                                                                                                                    * role assignment;
                                                                                                                                    * VA/freelancer instructions;
                                                                                                                                    * SOP generation;
                                                                                                                                    * automated status reporting;
                                                                                                                                    * integration with Trello, Gmail, Drive, Calendar, CRM, or other tools.
Example:
Free: “Create a manual task.”
Paid: “Turn this business objective into a 12-week execution plan with tasks, dependencies, owner roles, and weekly milestones.”
________________


10. High-value strategic outputs should be paid
Some outputs directly create business value and should clearly be paid.
Examples:
                                                                                                                                    * investor-readiness assessment;
                                                                                                                                    * fundraising strategy;
                                                                                                                                    * grant application strategy;
                                                                                                                                    * bank-loan preparation;
                                                                                                                                    * go-to-market strategy;
                                                                                                                                    * pricing strategy;
                                                                                                                                    * hiring plan;
                                                                                                                                    * freelancer brief;
                                                                                                                                    * MVP specification;
                                                                                                                                    * product roadmap;
                                                                                                                                    * customer validation strategy;
                                                                                                                                    * partnership strategy;
                                                                                                                                    * legal-risk preparation memo;
                                                                                                                                    * procurement or supplier comparison;
                                                                                                                                    * automation architecture plan.
These are not simple chatbot answers. They are venture-building assets.
________________


11. Professional-review packages should be paid
Changepreneurship itself should not pretend to replace lawyers, accountants, tax advisors, or regulated professionals. But it can prepare materials for professional review.
Paid examples:
                                                                                                                                    * lawyer review package;
                                                                                                                                    * accountant review package;
                                                                                                                                    * grant-advisor package;
                                                                                                                                    * municipal submission package;
                                                                                                                                    * bank review package;
                                                                                                                                    * insurance review package;
                                                                                                                                    * investor due-diligence pack;
                                                                                                                                    * technical-review pack;
                                                                                                                                    * freelancer handover pack.
The value is in organizing information clearly so a professional can review it faster.
________________


12. Data-room and stakeholder-sharing features should be paid
A basic user dashboard can be free, but serious data-room features should be paid.
Paid features may include:
                                                                                                                                    * external stakeholder portal;
                                                                                                                                    * investor data room;
                                                                                                                                    * shareable venture profile;
                                                                                                                                    * version-controlled documents;
                                                                                                                                    * access logs;
                                                                                                                                    * permission management;
                                                                                                                                    * watermarking;
                                                                                                                                    * downloadable ZIP packages;
                                                                                                                                    * structured evidence library;
                                                                                                                                    * professional review workspace;
                                                                                                                                    * partner/investor sharing links.
This creates storage, security, and business value, so it should not be fully free.
________________


13. Advanced storage should be paid
Small storage can be free. Large or long-term storage should be paid.
Paid storage may include:
                                                                                                                                    * large file uploads;
                                                                                                                                    * many saved projects;
                                                                                                                                    * long-term archive;
                                                                                                                                    * large document library;
                                                                                                                                    * video/audio processing;
                                                                                                                                    * scanned document processing;
                                                                                                                                    * version history;
                                                                                                                                    * data-room storage;
                                                                                                                                    * external stakeholder access.
The rule should be:
The user can control and access their basic data for free, but large-scale storage and processing should be paid.
________________


14. Regeneration and refreshes should be paid
Viewing an existing output should generally be free. Regenerating it should be paid.
Paid examples:
                                                                                                                                    * regenerate business plan;
                                                                                                                                    * update pitch deck with new data;
                                                                                                                                    * refresh market research;
                                                                                                                                    * rerun founder analysis;
                                                                                                                                    * rewrite investor memo;
                                                                                                                                    * reprocess uploaded documents;
                                                                                                                                    * update grant search;
                                                                                                                                    * regenerate financial narrative;
                                                                                                                                    * revise outreach campaign.
This is fair because regeneration consumes new resources.
Example:
Free: view previous business plan.
Paid: regenerate business plan using new market information.
________________


15. Integrations should be paid when they create value
Basic connection management should be free. Productive integration usage should be paid.
Paid integrations may include:
                                                                                                                                    * Trello automation;
                                                                                                                                    * Gmail processing;
                                                                                                                                    * Google Drive analysis;
                                                                                                                                    * Calendar scheduling;
                                                                                                                                    * CRM synchronization;
