But if his answer was one of thousands that helped the platform learn that “users with housing-related legal disputes often need help organising correspondence,” that general benchmark insight may remain, as long as it no longer contains Robert’s identity, wording, documents, location, case details, or any unique pattern that could identify him.
The platform should avoid “fake anonymisation”
The platform should not claim that data is anonymous when it is merely pseudonymised.
For example, replacing a user’s name with “User 48291” is not enough if the system can still reconnect that data to the user through account logs, timestamps, location, uploaded files, email history, or unique case facts.
Pseudonymised data remains personal data if it can be linked back to a person using additional information. (European Data Protection Board)
Therefore, benchmark data should only survive account deletion if the platform has removed:
                                                                                                                                             * User identifiers.
                                                                                                                                             * Account references.
                                                                                                                                             * Exact free-text responses.
                                                                                                                                             * Uploaded documents.
                                                                                                                                             * Unique case details.
                                                                                                                                             * Rare personal facts.
                                                                                                                                             * Timestamps that could identify the user.
                                                                                                                                             * External account references.
                                                                                                                                             * Any internal key that allows re-linking.
Recommended policy position
The platform should tell users this upfront:
“You can delete your personal data at any time. When you do, we delete your account, personal information, uploaded documents, connected-account access, raw answers, and identifiable journey history. If your responses have contributed to aggregated benchmark data, those benchmark insights may remain only when they have been irreversibly anonymised and can no longer be linked back to you. We do not keep identifiable benchmark contributions after deletion.”
This is the strongest and cleanest position.
It protects the user’s right to leave, while still allowing the platform to improve from general, anonymised learning.
Final answer
Yes, users should be able to delete all personal data connected to them.
When they do, their raw responses and identifiable benchmark contributions should be deleted or irreversibly anonymised. The platform may retain benchmark data only when it is aggregated, anonymised, and impossible to link back to the user.
In short:
Personal data should disappear. Anonymous statistical learning may remain. Identifiable benchmark contributions should not.
	 
SECTION 13   PRIORITY & SEQUENCING
 
You cannot build everything at once. This section forces prioritization.
13.1     CRITICAL 
If you had to launch with only 3 features, what would they be and why?
If the platform had to launch with only three features, I would choose the three features that create the full core loop of the product:
Understand the user → take action for the user → learn from the outcome.
Everything else can come later. The first version does not need to be beautiful, broad, or feature-rich. It needs to prove that the platform can help a user move forward in the real world.
The three essential launch features should be:
                                                                                                                                             1. Intelligent intake and user diagnosis
                                                                                                                                             2. External-account action engine
                                                                                                                                             3. Progress, outcome, and benchmark tracking
________________


1. Intelligent intake and user diagnosis
The first essential feature is a strong intake system that understands who the user is, what situation they are in, what they are trying to achieve, and what is blocking them.
This should not be a basic registration form. It should be a guided diagnostic process that builds a structured profile of the user.
The platform needs to understand things such as:
                                                                                                                                             * What stage the user is in.
                                                                                                                                             * What problem they are trying to solve.
                                                                                                                                             * What goal they are trying to reach.
                                                                                                                                             * What resources they currently have.
                                                                                                                                             * What skills, knowledge, network, or confidence they are missing.
                                                                                                                                             * Whether the user is realistically ready to take action.
                                                                                                                                             * Whether the user first needs stability, clarity, support, documentation, money, mentorship, or emotional reassurance.
                                                                                                                                             * Which external platforms or services may be useful for this user.
                                                                                                                                             * Which benchmark group the user belongs to.
This feature is critical because the platform cannot help the user properly without first understanding the user properly.
The intake should also be grounded in the idea that people do not all start from the same position. A person who is still struggling with survival, security, housing, finances, legal problems, or emotional stress cannot be treated the same as someone who is already stable and only needs business strategy.
This is where the Maslow-inspired view becomes important. The platform should recognise whether a user is ready for entrepreneurship, mentorship, networking, funding, business planning, or whether the user first needs to stabilise a more basic layer of life.
The purpose of this feature is not just to collect information. The purpose is to create the user’s starting point.
Without this feature, the platform becomes generic. It would give the same kind of advice to everyone, which would make it weak.
With this feature, the platform can say:
“This is where you are. This is what is blocking you. This is the next practical step.”
That is the foundation of the entire platform.
________________


2. External-account action engine
The second essential feature is the ability for the platform to take action through the user’s connected accounts on approved external platforms.
This is one of the most important parts of the concept.
The platform should not try to build everything itself from day one. It should not immediately build its own mentor network, its own marketplace, its own community, or its own funding database if strong external platforms already exist.
Instead, the platform should allow the user to connect accounts from accepted and legitimate external platforms. Then the platform can help the user use those platforms more effectively.
For example, if MicroMentor is accepted as a legitimate platform, the user could connect their MicroMentor account. The platform could then help the user:
                                                                                                                                             * Improve their profile.
                                                                                                                                             * Identify suitable mentors.
                                                                                                                                             * Draft outreach messages.
                                                                                                                                             * Send or prepare mentor requests.
                                                                                                                                             * Track responses.
                                                                                                                                             * Follow up professionally.
                                                                                                                                             * Compare which outreach strategies work best.
                                                                                                                                             * Recommend the next action based on the user’s goal and stage.
This feature is critical because it turns the product from an advice platform into an execution platform.
Many platforms only tell users what they should do. That is not enough. A lot of people already know roughly what they should do, but they do not move forward because they lack structure, confidence, language, persistence, timing, or the ability to navigate the right systems.
The stronger version of the platform is not:
“Here is what you should do.”
The stronger version is:
“Here is what needs to happen, and we will help you actually do it through the right channels.”
This also fits the philosophy of reducing the burden on the user. The platform should not make users responsible for constantly remembering, searching, comparing, writing, following up, and organising everything manually.
The platform should act as a practical assistant that helps the user move through external systems.
This feature would likely be the strongest proof that the platform is different from a normal chatbot, course, community, or static advice website.
________________


3. Progress, outcome, and benchmark tracking
The third essential feature is a system that tracks what happened after actions were taken.
The platform needs to know:
                                                                                                                                             * What advice was given.
                                                                                                                                             * What action was taken.
                                                                                                                                             * Which external platform was used.
                                                                                                                                             * Whether the user accepted or rejected the recommendation.
                                                                                                                                             * Whether a message was sent.
                                                                                                                                             * Whether someone replied.
                                                                                                                                             * Whether the user received help.
                                                                                                                                             * Whether the user moved forward.
                                                                                                                                             * Whether the user got stuck.
                                                                                                                                             * Which intervention worked.
                                                                                                                                             * Which intervention failed.
                                                                                                                                             * What the next recommended step should be.
This is critical because the platform should become smarter over time.
Without outcome tracking, the platform is blind. It can suggest actions, but it cannot know whether those actions actually helped.
With outcome tracking, the platform can build benchmark data.
For example, the system may learn:
“Users in this stage usually need three mentor outreach attempts before receiving a useful reply.”
Or:
“Users with incomplete profiles receive fewer responses.”
Or:
“Users who describe their problem in practical terms receive better mentor matches than users who describe it too generally.”
Or:
“Users who are still unstable financially often fail to complete business-development tasks unless the platform first helps them stabilise their basic situation.”
This benchmark layer is important because the platform should not only help one user at a time. It should learn from many users and improve the journey for future users.
This feature also creates accountability. The user can see what has been done, what is pending, what worked, and what still needs attention.
The progress system should be simple in the first version. It does not need to be a complex dashboard. It only needs to clearly show:
                                                                                                                                             * Current stage.
                                                                                                                                             * Current goal.
                                                                                                                                             * Recommended next step.
                                                                                                                                             * Actions taken.
                                                                                                                                             * Waiting items.
                                                                                                                                             * Results.
                                                                                                                                             * Next follow-up.
                                                                                                                                             * Main blocker.
This gives the user clarity and prevents the journey from becoming chaotic.
________________


Why these three features?
These three features are the minimum viable version of the platform because they form the complete product loop.
Feature 1: Diagnose
The platform understands the user and identifies the right next step.
Feature 2: Act
The platform helps the user take action through approved external accounts and platforms.
Feature 3: Learn
The platform tracks outcomes, improves recommendations, and builds benchmark intelligence.
Together, they create the essential loop:
Diagnose → Act → Measure → Improve → Repeat
That loop is more important than any individual feature.
The platform should not launch first with courses, community forums, gamification, advanced dashboards, payment systems, large mentor databases, or broad integrations. Those can all be added later.
The first launch should prove one thing:
Can the platform understand a user’s situation, help them take a real-world action, and learn whether that action worked?
If the answer is yes, the platform has a real foundation.
________________


What should not be part of the first launch?
If only three features are allowed, I would deliberately not prioritise:
                                                                                                                                             * A full internal mentor network.
                                                                                                                                             * A public community.
                                                                                                                                             * A large course library.
                                                                                                                                             * Gamification.
                                                                                                                                             * Complex social features.
                                                                                                                                             * A marketplace.
                                                                                                                                             * Advanced analytics dashboards.
                                                                                                                                             * Too many third-party integrations.
                                                                                                                                             * Native mobile apps.
                                                                                                                                             * AI-generated business plans as a standalone feature.
                                                                                                                                             * A large content library.
These things may be useful later, but they are not the core.
The core is not content.
The core is not community.
The core is not a dashboard.
The core is not a chatbot.
The core is guided execution.
The platform should help the user move from confusion to action, and from action to measurable progress.
________________


Final answer
If the platform could launch with only three features, they should be:
                                                                                                                                             1. Intelligent intake and user diagnosis
To understand the user’s situation, stage, blockers, readiness, and next logical step.
                                                                                                                                             2. External-account action engine
To help the user take real action through approved platforms such as MicroMentor, using the user’s own connected accounts where appropriate.
                                                                                                                                             3. Progress, outcome, and benchmark tracking
To measure what happened, guide follow-up actions, and improve the platform’s recommendations through anonymised benchmark learning.
These three features are enough to prove the platform’s real value.
They create the minimum complete system:
Understand the user, act on their behalf, track the result, and improve the next recommendation.
	 
 
13.2     CRITICAL 
What is the single thing that — if it doesn't work correctly — makes the entire platform worthless?  This becomes the top engineering priority.
The single thing that would make the entire platform worthless if it does not work correctly is:
The platform’s ability to take the right action on behalf of the user, safely, accurately, and with full traceability.
This is the top engineering priority.
The platform is not just an information tool. It is not only giving advice, writing plans, or explaining options. The real value of the platform is that it helps the user move forward by taking practical action through approved external platforms and connected accounts.
If that action layer does not work correctly, the platform loses its core purpose.
________________


Why this is the most critical point
The platform can survive an imperfect interface.
It can survive limited integrations.
It can survive having fewer mentor platforms at launch.
It can survive a basic dashboard.
It can even survive imperfect recommendations in the beginning, as long as they are clearly marked and improve over time.
But it cannot survive unsafe, incorrect, unreliable, or untraceable actions.
If the platform sends the wrong message, contacts the wrong person, applies through the wrong account, misrepresents the user, loses track of what it did, repeats actions by mistake, or acts without proper permission, then the user can no longer trust it.
And if the user cannot trust the platform to act on their behalf, the platform becomes worthless.
The entire concept depends on trust.
________________


The core risk
The biggest risk is not that the platform gives a bad suggestion.
The biggest risk is that the platform takes a real-world action incorrectly.
For example:
                                                                                                                                                * It sends a message to the wrong mentor.
                                                                                                                                                * It says something the user did not approve.
                                                                                                                                                * It applies to an external platform with incorrect information.
                                                                                                                                                * It sends duplicate outreach messages.
                                                                                                                                                * It changes a connected account in a way the user did not expect.
                                                                                                                                                * It loses the record of what was done.
                                                                                                                                                * It cannot explain why an action was taken.
                                                                                                                                                * It acts outside the boundaries the user agreed to.
                                                                                                                                                * It creates legal, reputational, financial, or personal damage for the user.
That would be fatal to the product.
A user may forgive a clumsy design.
A user may forgive a slow feature.
A user may forgive a limited first version.
But a user will not forgive a platform that acts irresponsibly in their name.
________________


Therefore, the top engineering priority is the trusted action system
The most important engineering priority should be a trusted action system.
This means the platform must have a reliable technical layer that controls every action the platform takes on behalf of the user.
This layer should answer the following questions every time:
                                                                                                                                                1. What action is being proposed?
                                                                                                                                                2. Why is this action being proposed?
                                                                                                                                                3. Which account or external platform will be used?
                                                                                                                                                4. What exact information will be submitted or sent?
                                                                                                                                                5. Has the user given permission for this type of action?
                                                                                                                                                6. Does this specific action require additional approval?
                                                                                                                                                7. Has this action already been performed before?
                                                                                                                                                8. What happened after the action was taken?
                                                                                                                                                9. Can the action be audited afterwards?
                                                                                                                                                10. Can the user understand and challenge what happened?
If these questions cannot be answered clearly, the action should not be executed.
________________


What “working correctly” means
For this platform, “working correctly” does not only mean that the software technically runs.
It means the platform acts according to strict rules.
A correct action must be:
1. Accurate
The platform must understand the user’s situation and take the correct action for that situation.
It should not blindly execute a generic recommendation.
For example, if the user needs a mentor, the platform should understand what kind of mentor is relevant, which platform is appropriate, and what message should be sent.
________________


2. Authorised
The platform must only act within the permissions given by the user.
Some actions may be safe to automate. Other actions may require explicit approval before execution.
For example:
                                                                                                                                                * Drafting a message may be automatic.
                                                                                                                                                * Sending a message may require approval.
                                                                                                                                                * Changing an account profile may require approval.
                                                                                                                                                * Submitting an application may require stronger confirmation.
The platform should never silently cross the boundary between assistance and representation.
________________


3. Transparent
The user must be able to see what the platform is doing.
The platform should not feel like a black box.
The user should be able to see:
                                                                                                                                                * What action was recommended.
                                                                                                                                                * Why it was recommended.
                                                                                                                                                * What data was used.
                                                                                                                                                * What message or submission was prepared.
                                                                                                                                                * Whether it was sent, saved, rejected, or postponed.
                                                                                                                                                * What the current status is.
Transparency is essential for trust.
________________


4. Traceable
Every important action must have an audit trail.
The platform should log:
                                                                                                                                                * The user’s goal.
                                                                                                                                                * The recommendation.
                                                                                                                                                * The user’s approval or rejection.
                                                                                                                                                * The connected account used.
                                                                                                                                                * The final content submitted or sent.
                                                                                                                                                * The timestamp.
                                                                                                                                                * The result.
                                                                                                                                                * Any follow-up needed.
This is important not only for debugging, but also for accountability.
If something goes wrong, the platform must be able to reconstruct what happened.
________________


5. Reversible where possible
Where possible, actions should be reversible or correctable.
For example:
                                                                                                                                                * Drafts can be edited.
                                                                                                                                                * Follow-up messages can clarify mistakes.
                                                                                                                                                * Profile changes can be reverted.
                                                                                                                                                * Failed actions can be retried carefully.
Not every action is reversible, which is exactly why high-impact actions need stricter approval.
________________


6. Safe by default
The platform should be conservative with action-taking.
When there is uncertainty, the platform should pause, ask for approval, or present the action as a draft.
A safe first version is better than an aggressive version that damages trust.
________________


What this means for engineering
The top engineering priority is not simply “build integrations.”
The priority is to build a safe and reliable execution architecture around integrations.
That includes:
                                                                                                                                                * Strong account connection and permission management.
                                                                                                                                                * Clear user consent flows.
                                                                                                                                                * Action preview before execution.
                                                                                                                                                * Approval gates for sensitive actions.
                                                                                                                                                * Action logs and audit trails.
                                                                                                                                                * Duplicate-action prevention.
                                                                                                                                                * Error handling and retry logic.
                                                                                                                                                * Rollback or correction options where possible.
                                                                                                                                                * Status tracking after every action.
                                                                                                                                                * Monitoring for failed or incomplete actions.
                                                                                                                                                * Security around connected accounts and tokens.
                                                                                                                                                * Clear separation between suggestions, drafts, and executed actions.
The platform should treat every external action as a serious event.
________________


The simplest rule
A simple rule for the engineering team should be:
No action may be executed unless the platform can prove what it is doing, why it is doing it, under whose authority it is doing it, and what happened afterwards.
This rule should guide the whole system.
________________


Why this matters more than the AI itself
The AI recommendation engine is important, but it is not the deepest engineering risk.
If the AI gives an imperfect recommendation but the platform shows it clearly and asks the user for approval, the damage can be limited.
But if the execution layer silently performs the wrong action, then even a good recommendation engine becomes dangerous.
So the platform should not be built as:
AI → action
It should be built as:
AI → proposed action → validation → user permission → execution → audit trail → outcome tracking
That is the difference between a useful assistant and an unsafe automation system.
________________


Final answer
The single thing that makes the entire platform worthless if it does not work correctly is the trusted action system: the platform’s ability to take actions on behalf of the user safely, accurately, with permission, and with full traceability.
If users cannot trust the platform to act correctly through their connected accounts, the entire value proposition collapses.
Therefore, the top engineering priority should be:
Build a safe, auditable, permission-based action layer before scaling features, integrations, dashboards, or advanced AI recommendations.
The platform only becomes valuable when it can reliably help users move forward in the real world.
But it only deserves to do that if every action is controlled, transparent, authorised, and traceable.
	 
 
13.3
What is acceptable to be rough or imperfect at launch?
At launch, it is acceptable for many parts of the platform to be rough, limited, or manually assisted, as long as the trusted action system works correctly.
The platform does not need to feel complete on day one. It does not need to support every external platform, every user type, every mentor network, every form of automation, or every advanced AI feature immediately.
What matters most is that the user can safely make progress, that the platform acts within clear boundaries, and that every meaningful action is transparent, authorised, and traceable.
In other words:
The launch version may be narrow, simple, and imperfect, but it must not be unsafe, confusing, or untrustworthy.
________________


What can be rough at launch
1. The user interface can be simple
The interface does not need to be beautiful or highly polished at launch.
It is acceptable if the first version looks functional rather than impressive, provided that users can clearly understand:
                                                                                                                                                * where they are in the journey;
                                                                                                                                                * what the platform recommends;
                                                                                                                                                * what action is being proposed;
                                                                                                                                                * what requires their approval;
                                                                                                                                                * what has already been done;
                                                                                                                                                * what the next step is.
The interface may be basic, but it may not be unclear.
A plain dashboard is acceptable.
A confusing dashboard is not.
________________


2. The number of supported external platforms can be limited
The platform does not need to connect to many external platforms at launch.
It is acceptable to start with a small number of approved, legitimate platforms, such as one or two mentor or business-support platforms.
The goal of launch is not to prove that the platform can connect to everything. The goal is to prove that the platform can connect to selected external accounts and use them responsibly on behalf of the user.
A narrow integration set is acceptable.
An unreliable integration layer is not.
________________


3. Some processes can be manually supported behind the scenes
At launch, not every step needs to be fully automated.
Some parts of the process may be handled manually or semi-manually, especially if that allows the team to learn faster and avoid unsafe automation too early.
For example:
                                                                                                                                                * reviewing AI-generated outreach before sending;
                                                                                                                                                * manually verifying mentor-platform profiles;
                                                                                                                                                * manually checking whether an external platform is legitimate;
                                                                                                                                                * manually reviewing edge cases;
                                                                                                                                                * manually helping users when an integration fails.
This is acceptable as long as the user experience remains honest.
The platform should not pretend that something is fully automated if it is actually supported manually. But manual assistance is acceptable in the early stage if it improves safety and learning.
________________


4. The AI recommendations can be basic
The AI does not need to be perfect at launch.
It is acceptable if the platform starts with relatively simple recommendation logic, provided that the recommendations are clearly presented as proposals rather than unquestionable decisions.
For example, the platform may say:
                                                                                                                                                * “This seems like the next useful step.”
                                                                                                                                                * “This mentor profile appears relevant.”
                                                                                                                                                * “This message draft may be suitable.”
                                                                                                                                                * “This platform may be worth using.”
That is acceptable.
What is not acceptable is for the AI to make high-impact decisions without explanation, validation, or user approval.
The AI may be imperfect.
The execution of actions may not be careless.
________________


5. The mentor-matching logic can be limited
The platform does not need to have a perfect mentor-matching engine at launch.
It is acceptable if the first version uses simple matching criteria, such as:
                                                                                                                                                * industry;
                                                                                                                                                * business stage;
                                                                                                                                                * language;
                                                                                                                                                * location;
                                                                                                                                                * user goal;
                                                                                                                                                * type of problem;
                                                                                                                                                * platform availability.
The first version only needs to be good enough to create meaningful progress.
The platform can improve matching quality over time by learning from:
                                                                                                                                                * which mentors respond;
                                                                                                                                                * which conversations are useful;
                                                                                                                                                * which recommendations users reject;
                                                                                                                                                * which outcomes lead to progress.
Basic matching is acceptable.
Random or misleading matching is not.
________________


6. The journey can be narrow
The platform does not need to support every possible entrepreneurial journey at launch.
It can begin with one clearly defined use case.
For example:
A user explains their situation, connects an approved external account, receives a recommended next step, approves outreach, and the platform helps them contact suitable mentors or support resources.
That is enough for an MVP.
A narrow but working journey is much better than a broad journey that breaks everywhere.
The first version should prove the core loop, not the entire vision.
________________


7. The content can be imperfect
The written guidance, educational content, explanations, and onboarding text can be improved over time.
It is acceptable if the content is not yet perfectly refined, as long as it is:
                                                                                                                                                * honest;
                                                                                                                                                * understandable;
                                                                                                                                                * not misleading;
                                                                                                                                                * not overpromising;
                                                                                                                                                * clear about limitations.
The platform does not need a complete knowledge library at launch.
It needs enough content to help users understand what is happening and make informed decisions.
________________


8. Reporting and analytics can be basic
Advanced analytics are not essential at launch.
It is acceptable if the platform only tracks the most important operational data, such as:
                                                                                                                                                * which actions were proposed;
                                                                                                                                                * which actions were approved;
                                                                                                                                                * which actions were executed;
                                                                                                                                                * which integrations failed;
                                                                                                                                                * which outreach messages received responses;
                                                                                                                                                * where users drop off.
Sophisticated charts, dashboards, and performance reports can come later.
However, the internal audit trail must not be rough. The platform must know what it did and why.
User-facing analytics can be basic.
Internal traceability must be strong.
________________


9. The onboarding can be imperfect
The first onboarding flow can be simple.
It does not need to capture every detail about the user immediately.
At launch, it is acceptable to ask only the most important questions needed to determine the first useful action.
For example:
                                                                                                                                                * What are you trying to achieve?
                                                                                                                                                * What problem are you facing right now?
                                                                                                                                                * What stage are you in?
                                                                                                                                                * Which external accounts are you willing to connect?
                                                                                                                                                * Do you want the platform to draft actions only, or also execute approved actions?
The onboarding can become more intelligent later.
The key is that the platform should not ask for unnecessary information before proving value.
________________


10. The design can lack polish
The brand identity, visual design, animations, and advanced UX details can be rough at launch.
The first users are more likely to care about whether the platform helps them move forward than whether every screen looks perfect.
However, the design must still support trust.
That means:
                                                                                                                                                * no misleading buttons;
                                                                                                                                                * no hidden consequences;
                                                                                                                                                * no unclear permissions;
                                                                                                                                                * no confusing approval flows;
                                                                                                                                                * no vague status messages.
A simple design is fine.
A design that causes users to misunderstand what they are approving is not.
________________


What may not be rough at launch
Some parts cannot be rough, even in an MVP.
These are non-negotiable.
1. Permissions
The platform must clearly know what it is allowed to do.
It must distinguish between:
                                                                                                                                                * drafting;
                                                                                                                                                * recommending;
                                                                                                                                                * preparing;
                                                                                                                                                * sending;
                                                                                                                                                * applying;
                                                                                                                                                * editing;
                                                                                                                                                * submitting;
                                                                                                                                                * following up.
The user must understand what they are authorising.
________________


2. Action approval
Any meaningful external action must have proper approval controls.
Especially when the platform acts through the user’s connected accounts, the user must be able to see what will happen before it happens.
________________


3. Traceability
The platform must keep a record of important actions.
It must be possible to answer:
                                                                                                                                                * what was done;
                                                                                                                                                * when it was done;
                                                                                                                                                * through which account;
                                                                                                                                                * based on which user goal;
                                                                                                                                                * with what content;
                                                                                                                                                * with what result.
This cannot be postponed.
________________


4. Security
Connected accounts, tokens, user data, and sensitive information must be handled securely from the start.
Security cannot be treated as a later improvement.
________________


5. Truthfulness
The platform must not pretend to do more than it actually does.
If a process is manual, limited, experimental, or semi-automated, that should be clear internally and, where relevant, to the user.
Early-stage products can be limited. They should not be deceptive.
________________


The correct launch mindset
The launch version should not try to be the full platform.
It should try to prove the smallest valuable version of the platform’s core promise:
The platform can understand a user’s goal, identify a useful next action, prepare that action, obtain the right permission, execute it through an approved external account, and show what happened afterwards.
Everything around that can be rough.
The product can start narrow.
The design can be simple.
The AI can be basic.
The integrations can be few.
The mentor matching can be imperfect.
Some operations can be manual.
But the core loop must work.
________________


Final answer
At launch, it is acceptable for the platform to be rough in areas such as design polish, number of integrations, onboarding depth, mentor-matching sophistication, advanced analytics, content quality, and full automation.
It is also acceptable to rely partly on manual or semi-manual processes while the team learns what users actually need.
However, it is not acceptable for the platform to be rough in the areas of permission, safety, traceability, user approval, connected-account handling, and action execution.
The launch version should therefore be:
Narrow, honest, safe, traceable, and useful — not broad, polished, and unreliable.
The MVP should prove one thing above all:
The platform can safely and responsibly help a user move from intention to real-world action.
	 
 
13.4
What would make you personally proud to show a user or an investor in the next 90 days?
What would make me personally proud is not a visually impressive demo that only looks good on the surface. I would be proud to show a working version of the platform that proves the core idea in a real, practical, and trustworthy way.
In the next 90 days, I would want to show that the platform can take a user from confusion to concrete action.
The user should be able to come in with a real problem, explain their situation, connect one or more approved external accounts, receive a clear recommendation, approve a proposed action, and then see that the platform has taken that action on their behalf in a transparent and traceable way.
That would make me proud because it would prove that the platform is not just another information tool. It would prove that it can actually help people move forward.
________________


The main thing I would be proud to show
The most important thing I would be proud to show is a working core loop:
A user explains their goal or problem, the platform understands the situation, identifies the next useful action, prepares that action, asks for permission, executes it through an approved external account, and shows the result afterwards.
That is the heart of the platform.
If we can show that clearly within 90 days, even in a narrow use case, then we have something meaningful.
It does not need to be broad yet. It does not need to support every type of entrepreneur, every mentor platform, every external account, or every possible situation.
But it does need to prove that the concept works.
________________


What I would want the user or investor to experience
I would want them to see a user journey like this:
                                                                                                                                                1. A user enters the platform with a real entrepreneurial challenge.
                                                                                                                                                2. The platform asks only the questions needed to understand the situation.
                                                                                                                                                3. The platform identifies that the user would benefit from external help, such as a mentor, advisor, support organisation, or relevant platform.
                                                                                                                                                4. The platform explains why that next step is recommended.
                                                                                                                                                5. The platform prepares a concrete action, such as a message, application, profile update, or outreach request.
                                                                                                                                                6. The user can review and approve the action.
                                                                                                                                                7. The platform performs the action through the user’s own connected account.
                                                                                                                                                8. The platform records what happened and shows the user the status.
                                                                                                                                                9. The user knows what the next step is.
That would be a strong 90-day result.
It would show that the platform is already capable of helping a user take action in the real world, not just giving generic advice.
________________


Why this would make me proud
It would make me proud because the platform would be solving one of the biggest practical problems many people face:
People often do not fail because they lack ideas. They fail because they do not know what to do next, who to contact, how to ask for help, or how to keep moving when things become unclear.
A platform that can reduce that friction has real value.
I would be proud if, within 90 days, we can show that the platform can help someone who is stuck become unstuck.
Not by giving them a motivational speech, but by helping them take a concrete next step.
________________


What I would want to show an investor
To an investor, I would want to show evidence that the platform has the beginning of a scalable model.
That means I would be proud to show:
                                                                                                                                                * a working product, not only designs;
                                                                                                                                                * at least one complete user journey;
                                                                                                                                                * one or more real external platform integrations;
                                                                                                                                                * clear permission and approval flows;
                                                                                                                                                * a traceable action history;
                                                                                                                                                * early user feedback;
                                                                                                                                                * evidence that users understand the value;
                                                                                                                                                * evidence that users would come back;
                                                                                                                                                * evidence that the platform can become more powerful as more integrations are added.
The investor does not need to see a finished company within 90 days.
But they should be able to see the direction clearly.
They should understand:
This platform is building an action layer on top of existing external platforms. It does not need to replace those platforms. It uses them on behalf of the user, with permission, to help the user make progress.
That is a much stronger story than simply saying, “We use AI to help entrepreneurs.”
________________


What I would want to show a user
To a user, I would want to show something practical and immediately understandable.
The user should not need a long explanation to understand the value.
They should be able to say:
“I came in with a problem. The platform helped me understand the next step. It prepared the message or action for me. I approved it. It sent or submitted it through my account. Now I can see what happened.”
That is the kind of experience that creates trust.
I would be proud if the user feels:
                                                                                                                                                * less alone;
                                                                                                                                                * less confused;
                                                                                                                                                * more guided;
                                                                                                                                                * more capable;
                                                                                                                                                * more likely to ask for help;
                                                                                                                                                * more likely to take action;
                                                                                                                                                * more confident that their progress is being organised.
The emotional result matters as much as the technical result.
The platform should not only perform actions. It should reduce the feeling of being overwhelmed.
________________


What I would not be proud of
I would not be proud of a demo that is beautiful but fake.
I would not be proud of:
                                                                                                                                                * a polished interface with no real action behind it;
                                                                                                                                                * an AI chatbot that only gives generic business advice;
                                                                                                                                                * a platform that claims to automate things but cannot actually execute anything;
                                                                                                                                                * integrations that are unstable or unsafe;
                                                                                                                                                * a journey where the user does not understand what is happening;
                                                                                                                                                * a product that looks impressive to investors but does not genuinely help users.
The 90-day goal should not be theatre.
It should be proof.
________________


The 90-day pride standard
The standard should be:
Can we show a real person using the platform to make real progress through a real external account, with clear approval and traceability?
If the answer is yes, then I would be proud.
Even if the design is simple.
Even if only one or two integrations work.
Even if some operations are still manual.
Even if the AI is not yet highly advanced.
Even if the journey is narrow.
A narrow working product is more valuable than a broad unfinished concept.
________________


A realistic 90-day version I would be proud of
A realistic version to be proud of could include:
1. A clear onboarding flow
The user can explain who they are, what they are trying to achieve, and where they are stuck.
The platform should not overwhelm the user with too many questions.
It should focus on understanding enough to recommend a useful next step.
________________


2. A first recommendation engine
The platform can identify whether the user needs information, structure, a mentor, outreach, a document, or another external action.
This does not have to be perfect yet.
But it should be good enough to create a useful first recommendation.
________________


3. One working external account integration
The platform should be able to connect to at least one legitimate external platform and perform a limited action through the user’s account.
For example, it could help the user prepare and send outreach through a mentor platform, business-support platform, or another approved platform.
This would prove the most important architectural idea.
________________


4. A user approval step
Before anything meaningful is done, the user can review the proposed action.
The user can approve, reject, or request changes.
This is essential because the platform is acting on behalf of the user.
________________


5. A traceable action log
The platform should show what action was proposed, what the user approved, what was executed, and what the outcome was.
This is important for trust, accountability, and future scaling.
________________


6. A simple but honest dashboard
The dashboard does not need to be advanced.
It only needs to show:
                                                                                                                                                * current goal;
                                                                                                                                                * recommended next step;
                                                                                                                                                * pending approvals;
                                                                                                                                                * actions already taken;
                                                                                                                                                * responses or results;
                                                                                                                                                * next suggested action.
That would already be valuable.
________________


What would make me especially proud
The thing that would make me most proud is seeing a user say something like:
“I normally would not have done this myself, but the platform helped me take the step.”
That would mean the platform is doing what it is supposed to do.
It is not just giving information.
It is not just producing text.
It is not just organising tasks.
It is helping the user cross the gap between intention and action.
That is the real value.
________________


Final answer
In the next 90 days, I would be personally proud to show a user or investor a working, narrow, honest version of the platform that proves the core promise.
The platform should be able to take a user from a real problem to a real-world action by understanding their situation, recommending a useful next step, preparing that action, getting the user’s approval, executing it through an approved external account, and showing the result afterwards.
I would be proud to show that the platform is not just another AI advice tool, but a trusted action layer that helps users move forward through existing platforms and accounts.
It does not need to be perfect within 90 days.
But it should prove that:
A user can come in stuck, and leave with meaningful progress already made on their behalf.
	 
SECTION 14   OPEN SPACE
 
Anything not covered above.
14.1
Is there anything about the vision for this platform that you have not been able to communicate to developers yet?  Write it in any form — bullet points, a story, a rough sketch described in words.
Robert, here is a stronger elaborated version that incorporates your idea clearly:
________________


14.1
Is there anything about the vision for this platform that you have not been able to communicate to developers yet?
Write it in any form — bullet points, a story, a rough sketch described in words.
Yes. One part of the vision that may still be difficult to communicate to developers is that Changepreneurship is not merely a business-planning platform, a chatbot, or a productivity tool.
At its core, Changepreneurship is intended to become a benevolent AI agent for venture building, backed by coordinated agent swarms that help users develop, structure, validate, launch, and sustain ventures.
The goal is to give users a true “venture building simplified” experience.
That means the platform should not only gather information, generate advice, or produce documents. It should help the user move from uncertainty to execution as quickly and safely as possible.
The platform should use AI agents, automation, integrations, and eventually robots or robotic process automation to do as much of the work as possible on behalf of the user, while still keeping the user in control of important decisions.
The vision is not:
“The user asks questions, and the AI gives answers.”
The vision is:
“The user has a venture they want to build, and the system helps carry as much of the practical burden as possible.”
This distinction is extremely important.
________________


The core vision
Changepreneurship should function as a trusted venture-building partner.
It should help the user with:
                                                                                                                                                * business development;
                                                                                                                                                * business planning;
                                                                                                                                                * market research;
                                                                                                                                                * mentor outreach;
                                                                                                                                                * customer discovery;
                                                                                                                                                * document preparation;
                                                                                                                                                * task execution;
                                                                                                                                                * follow-ups;
                                                                                                                                                * platform navigation;
                                                                                                                                                * opportunity identification;
                                                                                                                                                * operational structure;
                                                                                                                                                * accountability;
                                                                                                                                                * and long-term venture stability.
The platform should reduce the number of things the user personally has to figure out, write, search for, compare, remember, or execute.
In simple terms:
Changepreneurship should protect the user’s time and energy while helping them build a more stable venture in a shorter timeframe than was previously possible.
That is the deeper purpose.
________________


Why this matters
The world would benefit from more ventures.
Not only ventures that start, but ventures that survive, mature, and stay.
Many people have useful ideas, but they never turn those ideas into stable ventures because the process is too fragmented.
They have to:
                                                                                                                                                * research too much;
                                                                                                                                                * write too much;
                                                                                                                                                * compare too many options;
                                                                                                                                                * understand too many systems;
                                                                                                                                                * contact too many organisations;
                                                                                                                                                * find mentors;
                                                                                                                                                * structure business plans;
                                                                                                                                                * deal with forms, platforms, emails, and follow-ups;
                                                                                                                                                * stay motivated through uncertainty;
                                                                                                                                                * and make decisions without enough support.
This creates unnecessary friction.
As a result, many potentially valuable ventures never begin, or they collapse too early.
Changepreneurship should exist to reduce that friction.
________________


The platform is not just about information
A major risk is that developers may interpret the platform too narrowly.
They may think the product is mainly about aggregating information, creating dashboards, or generating AI-written business plans.
Those things are useful, but they are not the real product.
The real product is compressed execution.
The platform should help users achieve more progress with less personal effort.
That means it should not stop at:
“Here is what you should do.”
It should move toward:
“Here is the next useful action. I have prepared it. These are the reasons. These are the risks. Please approve, reject, or adjust.”
And eventually:
“Approved. I will now execute this through the correct channel and record the outcome.”
That is the experience I want developers to understand.
________________


Benevolent AI agent, not reckless automation
The word benevolent is important.
The platform should act in the user’s interest, but not take uncontrolled action.
It should be proactive, but not reckless.
It should be powerful, but not opaque.
It should execute work, but not remove the user’s authority.
It should simplify the journey, but not hide important consequences.
The basic operating principle should be:
The system proposes. The user approves. The agents execute. The system records. The venture moves forward.
This creates a balance between automation and trust.
The user should feel that the platform is capable enough to carry real workload, but transparent enough that they remain in control.
________________


Agent swarms as the hidden workforce
The platform should eventually be backed by specialised AI agents or agent swarms.
These agents could each have a role, such as:
                                                                                                                                                * a market research agent;
                                                                                                                                                * a business model agent;
                                                                                                                                                * a financial planning agent;
                                                                                                                                                * a mentor outreach agent;
                                                                                                                                                * a legal or compliance preparation agent;
                                                                                                                                                * a customer discovery agent;
                                                                                                                                                * a grant and funding search agent;
